sources.summary: NGC 4254 shows an increasing isotope ratio with radius and systematic internal variations, while lacking the bright barred-galaxy inner region seen in several targets.

quotes.quote: In the left panel of Figure fig:trends , we see ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O increase with increasing radius for NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 5055 and NGC 6946. Again NGC 628 represents the outlier, with a low ratio and perhaps a dropping rather than rising profile.

Three of our targets, NGC 3351, NGC 3627, and NGC 4321 host bright inner regions ( 500 pc, 0.05 r_ 25 ) likely fed by gas flows along the bars in these galaxies. No such features are apparent in NGC 4254, which lacks a strong bar.

In NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 5055 and NGC 6946 we do observe systematic internal variations. Here ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O increases by 40 with increasing radius. We also find a decreasing ^ 13 CO/C ^ 18 O ratio with increasing surface density of star formation.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4254 as a non-deficient Virgo object with a long HI tail and disk extensions, adopting its effective HI radius because of an anomalous isophotal diameter.

quotes.quote: Non-deficient galaxies are absent, because their negative deficiencies would require negative ram pressures. These include NGC 4254 despite its long HI tail.
NGC 4254 and NGC 4532 are non-deficient, but also have extensions of the HI disk.

The non-deficient NGC 4254 appears as a very odd object with R_ eff 30 R_ ISO , whose isophotal diameter is given as an exceptionally low value of 0.15 arcmin, obviously a typo. We take the R_ eff for the HI radius of this object.

sources.summary: EMPIRE identifies NGC 4254 as a Virgo unbarred, gas-rich target with a large HCN/CO decline and a central dense-gas fraction of 7 percent.

quotes.quote: It also covers strongly barred galaxies (NGC 2903, NGC 3627), flocculent disks (NGC 5055, NGC 6946), strong nuclear bursts (NGC 2903, NGC 4321, NGC 6946), Virgo cluster members (NGC 4254, NGC 4321), and interacting galaxies (NGC 3627, NGC 5194).

We observe the largest HCN/CO declines in NGC 3627 ( 0.60dex), NGC 4254 ( 0.60dex), NGC 5194 ( 0.60dex) and NGC 6946 ( 0.55dex). Again, we see no strong morphological divide, NGC 3627 and NGC 6946 are strongly barred galaxies with prominent dense gas emission in their centers and bars, while NGC 4254 and NGC 5194 are unbarred galaxies rich in molecular gas.

The recent higher resolution ( 8 500 pc) study of nearby galaxies presented by GALLAGHER18 , shows that a median value of 10 is characteristic of the inner kpc of four nearby galaxy disks (NGC 3351, NGC 3627, NGC 4254 and NGC 4321). Our EMPIRE central dense gas fractions are 9 , 7 and 10 for NGC 3627, NGC 4254 and NGC 4321, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4254 as a bright Virgo Cluster Sc galaxy with specified inclination, position angle, optical radius, distance, and stellar mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 4254 (M 99) is a bright Sc galaxy (morphological type code T = 5.2 0.7) in the Virgo Cluster. The inclination angle of NGC 4254 is i = 34 , the position angle of the major axis PA = 68 Lang2020 . The optical radius is 2.68 arcmin RC3 . At the distance of d = 13.1 Mpc Anand2021 , the physical optical radius is R_ 25 = 10.23 kpc. The stellar mass is M _ = 2.63 10 ^ 10 M _ Leroy2021 .

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 4254 in the high recent-SF-change group, says it lacks a bar, and cites a past rapid gravitational encounter.

quotes.quote: NGC 628, 4254, 4303, 4321, 4535, and 5068 belong to the high end of the distribution, while NGC 1365, 1512, 1566, 3351, and 3627 belong to its low end.This is also summarised in the last column of Table tab:yes_no . Looking at the distribution across the galactic disks in the 3 ^ rd column of Figure fig:all_maps , we observe large areas of increasing recent SF in NGC 628, 4254, 4303, and 4321, while NGC 1512, 1566, and 3627 exhibit recent SF activity that ranges from roughly unchanged to decreasing.

Galaxies with no confirmed or debated AGN include NGC 628 Liu_05, alston_21 , NGC 1512 Ducci_14 , NGC 3351 Gadotti_19 , NGC 4254 Burtscher_21 , NGC 4535 As per NED https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ , and NGC 5068 See Footnote footnote1 . We also note that it is likely that the presence of an AGN would not have a substantial effect on the SF activity, unless the AGN duty cycle is much longer than typically assumed. This is a result of the large differences between the dynamical timescales of the AGN and the galaxy as a whole. Galactic bar presence There are nine galaxies in the sample that have a galactic bar, as per the environmental masks in : NGC 1365, 1512, 1566, 3351, 3627, 4303, 4321, 4535, and 5068, while NGC 628 and NGC 4254 have no bar.
NGC 4254 is likely to have experienced a rapid gravitational encounter with another cluster member 280–750 Myr ago Vollmer_05, Boselli_18b .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4254 in the final spiral-galaxy dynamics analysis, finding non-gravitational gas-kinematic features and excluding a strictly maximal disk solution.

quotes.quote: We present results from a detailed dynamical analysis of five high surface brightness, late type spiral galaxies NGC 3810, NGC 3893, NGC 4254, NGC 5676, and NGC 6643 which were studied with the aim to quantify the luminous-to-dark matter ratio inside their optical radii. The galaxies' stellar light distribution and gas kinematics have been observed and compared to hydrodynamic gas simulations which predict the gas dynamics arising in response to empirical gravitational potentials, which are combinations of differing stellar disk and dark halo contributions.

We included NGC 4254 in the final analysis, which was published in KSR01. The analysis of NGC 4254 suffered from the high abundance of strong non-gravitationally induced gas kinematic features in the observed velocity field, which is apparent from the high abundance of small scale wiggles in the obseved rotation curves. The main conclusion for NGC 4254 was to exclude the strictly maximal disk solution.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4254 as a Virgo Cluster spiral with asymmetric polarized radio emission, using magnetic maps to link its field structure to cluster influence, density waves, and gas flows.

quotes.quote: The origin of asymmetric radio polarized emission in the Virgo Cluster spiral NGC 4254 is investigated and the influence of cluster environment on the properties of magnetic fields is explored. Structures of magnetic fields are analyzed with the concept of ``magnetic maps'', presenting distributions of different magnetic field components (total, regular, and random) over the entire galaxy, free of Faraday rotation and projection effects. A number of different physical phenomena influencing the magnetic field are modeled analytically and confronted with the galaxy's depolarization pattern and distribution of magnetic field strength obtained from multifrequency polarimetric radio observations. The study of orientation of intrinsic magnetic field vectors in NGC 4254 indicates that their dramatic variation (from 0 to more than 40 ) throughout the galaxy cannot arise from the dynamo process alone, but must be dominated by effects such as density waves and local gas flows.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4254 has relatively high star-formation efficiency, normal H-alpha star formation, asymmetric 24 micron morphology, and a uniform CO J=3-2/1-0 ratio.

quotes.quote: NGC 4254 appears to have a larger star formation efficiency(smaller gas depletion time), perhaps because it is on its first passage through the Virgo Cluster.

Morphologically, of the four Virgo spirals, NGC 4569 is the most centrally concentrated at 24 m, while NGC 4254 is both the least centrally concentrated and the most asymmetric b07 . The star formation distribution based on H emission is classified as normal for NGC 4254 but truncated/normal for the other three galaxies ky04a .
NGC 4254 has a remarkably uniform CO J =3-2/1-0 ratio of 0.33.

sources.summary: M99 is one of two Virgo spiral targets; the paper maps its ISM with Herschel/SPIRE and gas data, finding molecular dominance inside 80 arcsec and gas observations outperforming dust emission.

quotes.quote: M99 (NGC 4254) and M100 (NGC4321) are two spiral galaxies in the Virgo Cluster, which we take to have a distance of 16.8 Mpc.

In M99, the molecular material dominates the atomic gas within 80 arcsec of the galaxy centre and in M100 within 100 arcsec. We observed M99 and M100 during the Herschel Science Demonstration Phase with SPIRE.
Based on this criterion, mapping the ISM from the dust emission works best for M100 but mapping the ISM using the gas observations works best for M99.

sources.summary: For NGC 4254, the paper reports no extra thermal dust component, associates molecular gas and attenuation with FIR emission near star-forming regions, and finds high gas-surface-density correlations.

quotes.quote: A similar analysis on NGC 4254 and NGC 4321 (Bendo et al. 2013, in prep.) does not find such a dust component.

The slope is relatively similar for NGC 4254, NGC 4321, and NGC5194, but it is about twice as steep for NGC 2403. Conversely, NGC 2403 levels at a much lower face--on optical depth than average, whereas NGC 4254 and NGC 4321 level at a higher face--on optical depth.

We see that there is a tight correlation between the total gas surface density derived with a modified black body and from the combination of HI and CO observation as shown by the high value of the Spearman correlation coefficient for NGC 2403 ( =0.79 ), NGC 4254 ( =0.95 ), NGC 4321 ( =0.96 ), and NGC 5194 ( =0.95 ).

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4254 in its resolved SED sample, noting its HERACLES coverage, star-forming-sequence location, and status as the most massive, actively star-forming sample galaxy.

quotes.quote: Among the sample galaxies, two galaxies (NGC 4254 and NGC 4579) are not covered in the THINGS survey, although they are covered in the HERACLES survey. They are kept in our sample because the available molecular gas information still provides great insights into the gas properties.

NGC4254, NGC5194, and NGC 5457 reside on the star-forming sequence, while the other galaxies are located around the boundary between the star-forming sequence and green-valley (NGC 628, NGC 3627, and NGC 3184), and on the green-valley region (NGC 5055, NGC 3351, NGC 4736, and NGC 4579). NGC 4254 is the most massive and actively star-forming among the galaxies in our sample.

sources.summary: PHANGS-MUSE describes NGC 4254 as an average-mass flocculent star-forming disc where red diagnostic regions likely trace DIG and SNRs, and individual sources disappear at coarse resolution.

quotes.quote: These three galaxies represent the lowest stellar mass (NGC 5068), an average stellar mass flocculent morphology (NGC 4254) and the highest stellar mass, AGN-dominated, strongly barred morphology (NGC 1365).

In NGC 4254, a flocculent star-forming disc, the red regions are likely due to a combination of DIG emission and SNRs. Convolving our maps from the copt resolution ( 50 100 pc) to 150 pc and 500 pc scales, we see that the number and distribution of ionising sources changes with decreasing spatial resolution. Nearly all individual sources in NGC 4254 are lost at 500 pc.

Colouring each bin by the median luminosity-weighted age (centre row) reveals an expected correlation between younger ages and lower _ M_ (e.g. at larger radii) in the most disc-dominated galaxies (NGC 5068 and NGC 4254).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4254 as an unbarred Virgo Cluster spiral with a strong asymmetric spiral pattern, high star formation in its southern arm, and a long tidal tail.

quotes.quote: All of the galaxies in our sample for which devis2019 list metallicities have slightly sub-solar values of 12+ ( O/H ) , with the largest deficit being 0.136^ +0.070 _ 0.071 in NGC 4254 (compared to the solar value of 12+ ( O/H )=8.69 0.05 ; asplund2009 ), equivalent to Z/Z_ = 0.72^ +0.14 _ 0.10 .

NGC 4254 NGC 4254 (Messier 99), shown in Figure fig:ngc4254 , is an unbarred Virgo Cluster galaxy with a strong asymmetric spiral pattern which is clearly visible in our SCUBA-2 observations. NGC 4254 has a high star formation rate, particularly in its southern arm, and a tidal tail extending 250 kpc northward from the galaxy (not visible in our observations), suggesting that the galaxy is interacting with the centre of the Virgo Cluster haynes2007 . HARP ^ 12 CO J=3 2 observations of NGC 4254 were presented by wilson2009 and wilson2012 .

sources.summary: M 99 is presented as a radio-bright face-on Virgo spiral with disturbed morphology, past interaction signatures, and LOFAR evidence for ICM interaction.

quotes.quote: VCC 307 (NGC 4254, M 99) M 99 is the radio-brightest late-type galaxy in the Virgo cluster and oriented close to face-on. The high star-formation rate, the disturbed morphology with a peculiar spiral arm in the west and the long tail Haynes2007NGCSurvey is thought to originate from an encounter with another massive cluster member in the past Vollmer2005NGCCluster,Chyzy2007,Duc2008,Chyzy2008MagneticFields,Boselli2018 . However, even though the galaxy is located in the cluster outskirts at a distance of 1 Mpc to M 87, ram pressure stripping also appears to play a role Murphy2009 . In the LOFAR images and previous low-frequency studies Kantharia2008Low4254 , the radio emission extends beyond the optical disk towards the north for at least 10 kpc, this, together with the steep surface brightness gradient towards the south, is interpreted as a sign of interaction with the ICM while the galaxy is moving rapidly towards the cluster center.

sources.summary: NGC 4254 is modeled as a one-armed Virgo spiral whose disturbed H i and kinematics result from a rapid encounter followed by ongoing nearly face-on ram pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: Deep Effelsberg H i spectra of the one-armed, bright Virgo cluster spiral galaxy NGC 4254 are presented. Five different positions were observed in the 21 cm H i line with the Effelsberg 100-m telescope: one in the center and 4 located one HPBW to the NE, NW, SW, and SE, respectively, from the galaxy center. The spectra are compared to existing deep VLA observations, and the single dish and interferometric H i data are used to constrain a dynamical model which includes the effects of ram pressure. The peculiar, one-armed spiral pattern of NGC 4254 and its distorted and kinematically perturbed atomic gas distribution can be explained by a close and rapid encounter 280 Myr ago with another massive Virgo galaxy, followed by ram pressure stripping that is still ongoing.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4254 as a weakly interacting Virgo spiral whose encounter perturbed its arms, triggered star formation, and modified magnetic fields in a polarized ridge.

quotes.quote: NGC 4254 -- This is a weakly interacting spiral galaxy in the the Virgo cluster, which has recently experienced a gravitational encounter to have perturbed its spiral arms and triggered a burst of star formation (Chy zy et al. ). The galaxy shows a polarized emission throughout the disk, particularly strong in its southern ridge, shifted downstream of a density wave with up to 13 G as a regular magnetic field component and 20 G in the total field (Chy zy ). In the ridge, the dynamo-induced magnetic fields are modified by stretching and shearing forces, which are likely triggered off by tidal forces, which produced an anisotropic component of regular field and enhanced the polarized emission.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4254 as an isolated unbarred spiral with shallow metallicity gradients and no clear radial-profile breaks outside its inner regions.

quotes.quote: The final division of our sample yields two barred isolated galaxies (NGC 2903 5713), three unbarred isolated galaxies (NGC 0628, 3938, 4254), one barred weakly interacting galaxy (NGC 0337 There is some evidence that NGC 0337 might be undergoing an interaction or minor merger. See notes for column 8 in Table tab:venga-subsample . ), and one unbarred weakly interacting galaxy (NGC 5194).
The three isolated unbarred galaxies (NGC 0628, 3938, 4254) also show similarly shallow gradients of -0.44 0.13 in units of dex ( R / ) ^ 1 and -0.034 0.008 in units of dex kpc ^ 1 .
None of our unbarred isolated galaxies (NGC 0628, 3938, 4254) or unbarred interacting galaxy (NGC 5194) show any clear breaks in their radial profiles beyond the inner few kpc.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4254 among galaxies with common CO-only sightlines, spiral-arm offsets, and declining CO-only fractions at larger scales.

quotes.quote: Of the detected sightlines, CO only sightlines (dark blue) are the most common detections in more than half of our targets (namely NGC 0628, NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 4535, NGC 5194).
This is most evident along the spiral arms of NGC 0628, NGC 4321, and NGC 5194, but the same pattern can be seen in NGC 4254, and NGC 4535.
The exceptions are NGC 4254 and NGC 5194, where the CO only fraction continuously decreases from 140 pc to larger scales.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4254 as a perturbed Virgo spiral affected by tidal interaction and likely ram pressure, with a radio-bright southeastern disk and streaming-dominated transport.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, all perturbed Virgo spiral galaxies lie somewhat above the correlation: NGC 4522 (ram pressure stripping), NGC 4330 (ram pressure stripping), NGC 4396 (H i tail), NGC 4457 (probable minor merger), NGC 4532 (accreting H i filament), NGC 4501 (ram pressure stripping), and NGC 4254 (tidal interaction and probably ram pressure stripping).
The southeastern half of the galactic disk of NGC 4254 is radio-bright, whereas the northwestern half is radio-dim.
Streaming is the dominant transport mechanism in the disk of NGC 4321 and NGC 4254.

sources.summary: NGC4254 is one of the high-surface-density PHANGS disks; its separation-length profile peaks with radius, and its outer cloud-lifetime lower limit is among the most prominent.

quotes.quote: Extinction thus affects less than half of our sample (see Figure fig:gal_prop - galaxies that reside above this (highly conservative) threshold of 20 pc ^ 2 across more than 4 kpc in galactocentric radius are NGC3627, NGC4254, NGC4303, and NGC5194).

For example, \ is relatively constant in some galaxies (e.g. \ NGC628, NGC4303 and NGC4321), but in others it peaks at a certain radius (e.g. \ NGC3627, NGC4254 and NGC5194), or decreases outwards (e.g. \ NGC3351, NGC4535 and NGC5068).

This is most prominent in NGC4254, NGC3627 and NGC628 (lower limit on the cloud lifetime shown by the grey data point), but also somewhat in NGC5194 in the sense that the last bin falls above the median of all bins.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4254 is poorly reproduced by the mixing model, resides on Virgo Cluster outskirts, shows H I evidence for interaction, and has high ionized-gas velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: NGC 1087 and NGC 4254 in particular appear to be systematically poorly modeled, with the correlation scale overpredicted by a factor of three. NGC 4254 resides in the outskirts of the Virgo cluster, and shows strong evidence in the H I distribution of a recent interaction Vollmer2005 . Perhaps driven by these external dynamical effects, the ionized gas velocity dispersion across the galaxy is particularly high (Figure fig:pars2 , left), and drives the model towards longer correlation scales.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4254 as slightly molecule-rich, with evidence for a recent fly-by interaction, molecular bar, and asymmetric dense-gas spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 4254 appears slightly molecule-rich at fixed . There is evidence that it recently underwent a fly-by interaction vollmer05,haynes07,boselli18a , and it has a molecular bar and asymmetric spiral arms containing dense molecular gas sofue03 . These differences between the control sample and NGC 4651 and NGC 4254 highlight how environmental mechanisms can influence galaxies before they become significantly poor, and the importance of selecting a control sample from field galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4254 as a perturbed Virgo galaxy with high radio luminosity relative to SFR, weak polarization-radio/SFR correlation, and strong radio/SFR-SFR correlation.

quotes.quote: Many of the perturbed Virgo cluster galaxies (NGC 4522, NGC 4457, NGC 4402, NGC 4532, NGC 4501, and NGC 4254) are located at the upper end of the observed range of radio luminosities with respect to the SFR.

The two perturbed galaxies NGC 4254 and NGC 4654, which show a weak or absent correlation between the surface brightness of polarized radio continuum emission and the radio/SFR ratio, present a moderate or strong ( > 0.5) correlation between the fractional polarization (FP) and the radio/SFR ratio (second column of Table tab:coeffs2 ).

On the other hand, the radio/SFR ratio is only correlated with the SFR ( | | > 0.5 ) in NGC 6946, NGC 4254, and NGC 4654 and these are the galaxies where the radio/SFR ratio is best correlated with FP.

sources.summary: NGC 4254 is described as a luminous spiral with an odd one-armed morphology; models require a recent close encounter with a massive perturber, identified with VIRGOHI 21.

quotes.quote: Moreover the disc has unquestionably interacted with NGC 4254, a luminous spiral with an odd one-armed morphology, but lacking the massive interactor invariably responsible for such a feature. Published numerical models Vollmer05 of NGC 4254 call for a close interaction 10^8 years ago with a perturber of 10^ 11 solar masses. This we take as completely independent evidence for the massive nature of VIRGOHI 21.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 4254 at the Virgo periphery, cites prior structure, kinematic, polarization, and magnetic-field work, and finds strong radio/H i asymmetry with northern extensions.

quotes.quote: This galaxy is at the periphery of the Virgo Cluster. Its structure and kinematics were recently studied by Vollmer et al. ( ); its radio polarisation and magnetic field structure were investigated by Soida et al. ( ). Its strongly asymmetric structure is evident in both the radio continuum and the H i , with faint extensions to the North that go well beyond the optical disk.

gmw_00000587.name: NGC 821
gmw_00000587.overview: NGC 821 is an isolated E6 elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1209.4351, arXiv:2104.06396] that exhibits photometric and kinematic signatures of an edge-on stellar disk [arXiv:1209.4351, arXiv:1312.1337]. It hosts an inactive central black hole [arXiv:astro-ph/0701639] and a dark matter halo whose mass and profile remain a subject of debate [arXiv:0803.3626, arXiv:1209.4351].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 821 with HET stellar kinematics, finding a massive, shallow dark halo inconsistent with earlier planetary-nebula claims of little or no dark matter.

quotes.quote: We present line-of-sight stellar velocity distributions of elliptical galaxy NGC 821 obtained to approximately 100 (over 2 effective radii) with long-slit spectroscopy from the Hobby-Eberly Telescope. Our measured stellar line-of-sight velocity distributions are larger than the planetary nebulae measurements at similar radii. We fit axisymmetric orbit-superposition models with a range of dark halo density profiles, including two-dimensional kinematics at smaller radii from SAURON data. Within our assumptions, the best-fitted model gives a total enclosed mass of 2.0 10^ 11 M_ within 100 , with an accuracy of 2 ; this mass is equally divided between halo and stars.

At 1R_e the best-fitted dark matter halo accounts for 13 of the total mass in the galaxy. This dark halo is inconsistent with previous claims of little to no dark matter halo in this galaxy from planetary nebula measurements. We find that a power-law dark halo with a slope 0.1 is the best-fitted model; both the no dark halo and NFW models are worse fits at a greater than 99 confidence level. NGC 821 does not appear to have the expected dark halo density profile.

sources.summary: The survey describes NGC 821 as an isolated E6 galaxy with debated dark matter content, significant GC colour bimodality, extended GC surface density, and blue-GC minor-axis rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 821 is an isolated E6 galaxy Vaucouleurs with photometric and kinematic signatures of an edge-on stellar disk ( Emsellem07 ; Proctor ). The dark matter content of this galaxy has been debated in the literature. Romanowsky03 , and recently Teodorescu found that the velocity dispersion of the PNe decreases with radius. In contrast, Weijmans , Forestell and Proctor found a flat velocity dispersion for the stellar component within 100 arcsec.

However, we detect significant GC colour bimodality, although the blue and the red peaks are not clearly visible. We confirm that the surface density profile of the GC system extends up to 4 arcmin, as suggested from the WIYN imaging.

We detect significant rotation only for the blue GCs and for a small group of red GCs. The blue GCs are found to rotate at 85 along PA _ kin =85^ +26 _ 27 deg, consistent with the photometric minor axis and in agreement with that found by Coccato using PNe.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 821 as a misaligned isolated elliptical with disk evidence, minor-axis tracer rotation, strong stellar-population gradients, and a likely multiple minor-merger history.

quotes.quote: NGC 821 is an isolated elliptical galaxy classified as E6 according to NED The NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) is operated by the Jet Propulsion Laboratory, California Institute of Technology, under contract with the National Aeronautics and Space Administration. . However, Emsellem2004 found evidence of a rapidly rotating disk-like component from stellar kinematic studies, as part of the SAURON project. Further evidence of the presence of a stellar disk in NGC 821 comes also from earlier photometric studies, e.g. Michard1994,Goudfrooij1994,Ravindranath2001 as well as from stellar population and kinematic studies Proctor2005,Proctor2009 .

NGC 821 has a roughly flat stellar rotation velocity profile within 0.5 R_ e (from ATLAS ^ 3D ), which decreases out to 1 R_ e and, then, it remains roughly constant (from SLUGGS). The PNe show rotation between 1 2 R_ e , followed by a subsequent decrease out to large radii, i.e. 4 R_ e . The rotation velocity profile of the PNe is consistent with that of the stars at 1 R_ e , however the PNe are rotating more closely aligned to the photometric minor-axis of the galaxy with PA _ kin 150 at all radii, while the stars show rotation along the galaxy photometric major-axis out to 1 R_ e . A similar misalignment between the PA _ kin of the PNe and the PA _ phot of the galaxy was previously also found in Coccato2009 .

For NGC 821, we, thus, propose that the galaxy formed through multiple gas-poor minor (at least two) mergers in the mass-ratio range 1:4.5-1:10 in more recent times (i.e. z 1 ), similarly to NGC 4697.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 821 as a nearby elliptical with an inactive central black hole, Chandra-detected X-ray binaries, a faint hard nuclear source, little hot gas, and a supernova-driven wind.

quotes.quote: The relatively nearby (distance=24.1 Mpc) elliptical galaxy NGC821, hosting a central massive black hole but inactive at all wavelengths, was observed with Chandra for a total exposure of 230 ksec, to search for nuclear emission and gas available for accretion. Within its optical image, 41 sources were detected, with spectral properties typical of low mass X-ray binaries (LMXBs). The fractions of LMXBs in the field and in globular clusters were determined, together with their X-ray luminosity function (XLF) down to L(0.3--8 keV)=2 10^ 37 erg s ^ 1 . At the galactic center a source of L(0.3--8 keV)= 6 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 was detected for the first time, slightly extended. Its spectral shape is quite hard ( =1.49^ +0.14 _ 0.13 ), without intrinsic absorption.

Different lines of investigation consistently provide no evidence for hot gas. Hydrodynamical simulations show that stellar mass losses are driven out of NGC821 in a wind sustained by type Ia supernovae, but also hot accreting gas within a very small inner region.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 821 as an early-type example with sharply declining outer rotation, showing that central rotation does not reliably estimate total stellar angular momentum.

quotes.quote: This diversity is not simply a matter of inclination, as can be seen by the divergent cases of NGC 821 and NGC 2768, which are both highly flattened and probably close to edge-on. We thus find that the central rotation properties of early-type galaxies cannot be used to reliably estimate the total angular momentum content, and there is probably no simple function that universally characterizes their full rotation-velocity profiles. As with the spirals, we fit power laws to the outer regions of the rotation data in order to extrapolate to larger radii (see Appendix sec:obsapp for further details).

To see that the low characteristic rotation velocities for ellipticals are not a mathematical sleight of hand, one may consider the specific cases of NGC 821 and NGC 3377 in Figure  fig:etg . The rotation-velocity profiles of these galaxies decline dramatically outside x  (1--2)  , which may be contrasted with the spiral galaxies in Figure  fig:spirals . Preliminary analysis of additional edge-on cases, where the deprojection uncertainties are minimized, indicates that such declines are a generic feature of M^* early-type galaxies (A. Romanowsky et al., in preparation).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 821 as having declining planetary-nebula velocity dispersion, while polytropic dark-halo models improve over stars-only fits and favor F_d about 8.

quotes.quote: NGC 821 The kinematics of planetary nebulae around NGC 821 show a radially declining velocity dispersion romanowsky2003 . This has been taken as evidence disfavouring the presence of a dark halo (unless the PN orbital anisotropy has significant radial variations) or else the halo is less concentrated than CDM expectations. On both axes of the coccato2009 data, we find that polytropic halo models fit significantly better than stars alone. The improvement is best on the first axis. The best fits ( ^2_r 1.7 ) are for F_ d 8 and an outer radius 60 R_ h /R_ e 90 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC821 lacks detected hot X-ray gas; its unresolved X-ray light follows the stellar light and likely comes from faint compact objects, with possible low-density outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: The sample galaxies, NGC821, NGC3379, NGC4278, and NGC4697, have approximately identical K-band luminosities, and hence stellar masses, yet their X-ray appearance is strikingly different. We conclude that the unresolved emission in NGC821 and NGC3379 is built up from a multitude of faint compact objects, such as coronally active binaries and cataclysmic variables. Despite the non-detection of X-ray gas, these galaxies may host low density, and hence low luminosity, X-ray gas components, which undergo a Type Ia supernova (SN Ia) driven outflow.

Figure fig:profile demonstrates that in two galaxies, NGC3379 and NGC821, the X-ray light is well traced by the stellar light distribution at all radii. The good agreement between the X-ray and K-band profiles hints that the unresolved X-ray emission in these galaxies has a stellar origin, that is, it originates from the multitude of faint compact objects.

sources.summary: NGC 821 is treated as a disky elliptical or possible S0 whose inner rotation fades outward while dispersion and h4 maps show major/minor-axis structure.

quotes.quote: This E6 galaxy is a classic example of a disky elliptical Lauer:1985tk , although Kormendy:2013 argued for a reclassification as an S0. Its characteristic disky isophotes disappear beyond 1 \ (about 30 arcsec; Bender:1988vj,Nieto:1991vh,Goudfrooij:1994vj ). Rotation is prominent inside of this radius Dressler:1983dv,Bender:1994vo,Pinkney:2003jb,Emsellem:2004kr , but diminishes farther out (see Figure fig:skims0 ; Proctor:2009iy,Coccato:2009je,Weijmans:2010bn ). Our data show that both the velocity dispersion and the h_4 maps exhibit large-scale structure with distinctly different amplitudes along major and minor axes. Only the metal-poor GCs show significant rotation and that is along the photometric minor axis in the inner parts Pota:2013en , consistently with the PN data Coccato:2009je .

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 821's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 821: KormendyHo2013 quote B/T=0.95 , therefore we ignore the possible disk component and use the whole galaxy profile Graham2001 , getting R_e=34.8'' . Beifiori2012 report R_e=85.4'' and Laesker2014 'best fit' gives 3.5'' . The fit of Beifiori2012 overestimates the measured profile at radii larger than 70'' by 0.4 mag. Laesker2014 consider a bulge, a (faint) disk and a halo and it is not clear that bulge and halo should be treated as separate components. The M/L is scaled to the distance of KormendyHo2013 and from I to R- band using the mean (R-I) color from Hyperleda.

sources.summary: NGC 821 is one of the elliptical galaxies modeled with planetary-nebula dynamics; the authors note weak statistics, but its dark-halo fit gives substantial dark mass.

quotes.quote: Surprisingly, little dark matter were found contained in the three luminous elliptical galaxies (NGC 821, NGC 3379, NGC 4494) and the data is close to the model by Newtonian dynamics. The ``lack of dark matter'' can be explained by another view of the ``missing mass problem''. The crux of the matter is ``mismatch in acceleration''. This can be explained by unseen mass or modified theory of gravity. Milgrom&Sanders03 showed that those luminous elliptical galaxies reported by Romanowsky03 can be well explained by Modified Newtonian Dynamics (MOND).

From the table, we learn that, except NGC 821, the mass of other three elliptical galaxies matches well with the population synthesis model based on Salpeter IMF Cappellari13b . We point out that the statistics of NGC 821 is not very good due to insufficient stellar and PN data.

Within the dark matter halo model five galaxies (NGC 821, NGC 3379, NGC 4374, NGC 5846 and NGC 1344) have dark halo mass M_ dm (mass of dark matter within 5R_ eff ) significantly larger than the baryonic mass M_ h .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 821 as one of three intermediate-luminosity ellipticals whose PN.S planetary-nebula velocities reach 4--6 effective radii and show declining outer velocity dispersion inconsistent with simple dark-halo models.

quotes.quote: Observations with the PN.S on the 4.2-m William Herschel Telescope have allowed us to extend stellar kinematic studies to the outer parts of three intermediate-luminosity elliptical galaxies, NGC 821, NGC 3379, and NGC 4494 (Table 1). In each of these systems, we have measured 100 PN velocities with uncertainties of 20 km s ^ 1 out to radii of 4--6 R_ eff (Figs. 1--3). The line-of-sight velocities in the outer parts of all these galaxies show a clear decline in dispersion with radius (Fig. 4). A decrease in random velocities with radius has been indicated by small samples of PNe around NGC 3379 , but the more extensive data set presented here provides a more definitive measurement of this decline, and reveals that it also occurs in other similar galaxies. The new data are inconsistent with simple dark halo models (Fig. 4) and thus different from kinematical results for brighter ellipticals [ e.g., ] .

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes NGC 821 in a quiescent early-type galaxy sample, emphasizing its non-detected core, jet-like nuclear X-ray substructure, and low radio-core upper limit.

quotes.quote: The remaining two galaxies have already been studied in detail (NGC 821: Fabbiano et al. 2004; NGC 4697: Sarazin et al. 2001): here we re-analyse and discuss their nuclear X-ray properties in the context of our new sample of massive, quiescent elliptical galaxies. In the remaining three galaxies, NGC 3377, NGC 5845 and NGC 821, structure in the nuclear region is more clearly visible. In the other two galaxies, NGC 821 and NGC 3377, the emission has instead an elongated, ``jet-like'' appearance.

For a discussion of the elongated substructure in the nucleus of NGC 821, see Fabbiano et al. (2004). In NGC 821, the radio-core flux at 5 GHz is < 0.3 mJy (Wrobel Heeschen 1991); in NGC 3377, NGC 4697 and NGC 5845, the radio flux at 8.4 GHz is < 0.1 mJy (Krajnovic Jaffe 2002).

sources.summary: The paper characterizes the globular-cluster system of the nearby isolated E6 elliptical NGC 821, emphasizing its unusual value as an isolated comparison to cluster ellipticals.

quotes.quote: The present work aims to characterise the GC system hosted by the nearby, isolated elliptical NGC 821. Observational constraints gleaned from this GC system are an important contribution as nearly all previous work examined GC systems in the high-density environments of galaxy groups and clusters. NGC 821 is an elliptical (E6) galaxy (M _V = -21.0 ; de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991) with a surface brightness fluctuation (SBF) distance modulus of 31.75 0.17 ( 22 Mpc; Tonry et al. 2001; corrected according to Jensen et al. 2003). It reveals a small edge-on disc both photometrically (Lauer 1985) and kinematically (Emsellem et al. 2004).

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC0821, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC0821 (Fig. fig:NGC0821 ) is a typical lenticular galaxy hosting a highly-inclined disk (see subsec:beyond_b+d ). The disk is clearly visible by inspecting the images, and is reflected in the ellipticity and B4 harmonic coefficient profiles, as well as in the residuals of a single- \ model. While modeling this galaxy requires a disk component, the ``standard" bulge+disk configuration is fraught with problems.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 0821 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This is a field E6 galaxy. The ATLAS^ 3D velocity field 2004MNRAS.352..721E maps the inner regions of the galaxy where it behaves like a regular rotator along the photometric major axis. The PNe sample, instead, probes the kinematics from 1 to 5 and show a sustained rotation along the photometric minor axis with a maximum amplitude of 51 16 , suggesting the triaxiality of the object. No rotation is detected along the major axis.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 821 dark halo estimates, a weakly active nuclear black hole, and evidence for a jet while noting the adopted halo is more massive than previous work.

quotes.quote: NGC 821. There are two recent studies on the DM halo of this galaxy, providing large radii data.

The nuclear supermassive black hole in NGC 821 has been detected as a weak X-ray source, implying a very weak level of activity L_ X /L_ Edd 10^ 8 ; Pellegrini:2007 . There is also evidence for the presence of a jet Fabbiano:2004,Pellegrini:2007 .

sources.summary: The paper reports poor-seeing g-band imaging for NGC 821, says its measured GC sample is too small for bimodality testing, and notes most CaT-measured clusters are bright.

quotes.quote: We note that the g band imaging in NGC 821 and NGC 5846 and B band imaging from NGC 4278 were observed in poor seeing.
NGC 821, NGC 1400 and NGC 7457 do not have enough measured GCs to determine whether their distributions are bimodal or not.

In NGC 821, NGC 1400, NGC 1407 and NGC 5846, among the most distant galaxies in this study, the majority of GCs with CaT measurements are brighter than M_i = -10 while in NGC 3115 and NGC 3377, the closest galaxies, GCs as faint as M_i = -8 have CaT metallicities.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 821 as an observed early-type galaxy whose PNe and red globular clusters trace a velocity-dispersion bump, with an isolated E6 morphology and edge-on stellar disk signatures.

quotes.quote: We include in Figure fig:proj the observed galaxies which show a bump at the location of the bump of our simulated galaxy 11 OBH 13: For NGC 821, both PNe and red GCs trace a bump behavior between 0.5 and 1.8 ( = 5.79 kpc, PNe data from coccato:2009MNRAS.394.1249C , GCs data from pota:2013MNRAS.428..389P , stellar data from proctor:2009MNRAS.398...91P,forestell:2010ApJ...716..370F ). It is an isolated E6 galaxy, with a velocity dispersion that generally shows a rapid decrease with radius romanowsky:2003Sci...301.1696R and kinematic and photometric signatures of an edge-on stellar disk proctor:2009MNRAS.398...91P .
Observations of some ETGs such as NGC 821, NGC 1407, NGC 3115, NGC 4278 and NGC 4697 show a positive deviation of the velocity dispersion in the same range as our galaxies.

gmw_00000588.name: NGC 3377
gmw_00000588.overview: NGC 3377 is a bright, fast-rotating elliptical galaxy in the Leo I group [arXiv:1209.4351, arXiv:2104.06396] that features an embedded stellar disk [arXiv:2104.06396, arXiv:1310.2607] and a nuclear X-ray jet [arXiv:1011.5077, arXiv:astro-ph/0511293]. The galaxy is surrounded by a bimodal globular cluster system [arXiv:1209.4351, arXiv:2104.06396] and hosts a companion galaxy, NGC 3377A, to its northwest [arXiv:1209.4351, arXiv:2104.06396].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3377 as the closest sample elliptical, with old resolved stars, bimodal GC colours, extended GC kinematics, and red-GC rotation with a twisting kinematic angle.

quotes.quote: NGC 3377 is a classical E6 elliptical in the Leo I group and the closest galaxy in our sample. The resolved stellar properties of NGC 3377 have been studied by Harris07A that found no evidence for any young ( < 3 Gyr) stellar population. Coccato studied 159 PNe in this galaxy, detecting significant rotation within 2 arcmin and a kinematic major axis twist of 80 deg from the major towards the minor axis. Kundu , Chies-Santos and Cho studied the GC system of this galaxy using HST imaging, finding the colour distribution to be likely bimodal.

We found a high probability for the colour distribution to be bimodal, both in our Suprime-Cam and in the supplementary ACS archive imaging, as already found in Chies-Santos . In this work, we present radial velocities for 126 GCs, observed in 4 DEIMOS masks. It is worth noting that we extend the kinematics of this galaxy to 8 \ 25 kpc, two times further than the PNe studies.

We detect significant rotation only for the blue GCs and for a small group of red GCs. The blue GCs are found to rotate at 85 along PA _ kin =85^ +26 _ 27 deg, consistent with the photometric minor axis and in agreement with that found by Coccato using PNe. Such a peculiarity is clearly visible in Figure fig:CV . We will discuss this feature and its implications in more detail later. Interestingly, we note that the direction of the GC and PNe rotation coincides with an elongated jet/outflow structure detected in the X-ray Pellegrini . With the adopted colour split, the kinematic position angle of the red GCs is generally unconstrained, except the outer red GCs that counter rotate with respect to the host galaxy stars. The velocity dispersion of both the red and the blue GCs declines with radius with a slope similar to that of the PNe and host galaxy stars. NGC 1400 NGC 1400 has been classified both as a face-on S 0 Jarrett and as an E0 (e.g., daCosta ). It is the second brightest galaxy in the Eridanus group after NGC 1407 . We have assumed that NGC 1407 and NGC 1400 lie at the same distance of 26.8 Mpc. NGC 1400 is characterised by a uniformly old stellar age up to 1.3 \ Spolaor and by an unusually low systemic velocity (V _ sys = 558 ) for its distance. Forbes06A studied the GC system of this galaxy using Keck/LRIS in imaging mode, detecting significant bimodality. The photometric GC selection was performed within 8 arcmin from the galaxy centre to minimise the contamination from NGC 1407 GC system, resulting in a clear bimodal distribution, with the colour separation occurring at (g-i)=0.98 . The GC surface density was corrected for the local NGC 1407 contribution. This galaxy shows the steepest red surface density profile among our galaxies, similar to the slope of the galaxy surface brightness. In contrast, the profile of the blue GC subpopulation is more radially extended and also requires a power-law fit, because a S ersic function does not return a satisfactory solution. We present here radial velocities for 34 blue and 37 red GCs respectively, for a total of 71 spectroscopically confirmed GCs. Despite their small angular separation ( 10 arcmin), NGC 1407 and NGC 1400 also have a large peculiar velocity difference ( 1200 ), assuring a reliable separation of their spectroscopically confirmed GCs. The red GCs mimic the rotation of the stars in the inner regions, whereas the rotation of the blue GCs is consistent with zero with a marginal signature of counter-rotation in the inner radial bin. The velocity dispersion of the red GCs is in agreement with long--slit data in the region of overlap, with a slightly increasing trend towards the outer regions. Conversely, the velocity dispersion of the blue GCs decreased with the radius. NGC 1407 NGC 1407 is a massive E 0 galaxy at the centre of the dwarf galaxy dominated Eridanus A group Brough . It shows moderate rotation along the photometric major axis and has a weak central AGN Zhang . The stellar population analysis of SpolaorB found the galaxy to possess a uniformly old age within 0.6 . A dynamical analysis of NGC 1407 was given by Romanowsky09 . They used Suprime-Cam imaging and DEIMOS spectra for 172 GCs and found a massive dark halo. In this work, we use the photometric results presented in Romanowsky09 in which the Suprime-Cam imaging was reduced and analysed with the same methodology described in this paper. The colour distribution shows clear bimodality with the colour separation occurring at (g - i)=0.98 . We supplement the spectroscopic sample of Romanowsky09 with 6 additional DEIMOS masks, that make NGC 1407 , with a total of 369 spectroscopically confirmed GCs, the most populous spectro-photometric dataset in our galaxy sample. We detect rotation along the photometric major axis for both GC subpopulations. The blue GCs rotate in the innermost radial bin along the photometric minor axis and between 3 and 5 \ along the photometric major axis. Similarly, the rotation of the red GCs occurs along the photometric major axis directions, but in this case the rotation signal is generally larger and better constrained than that of the blue GCs. Both GC subpopulations appear to rotate in the outermost regions of the galaxy along the major axis of the galaxy. The velocity dispersion of the red GC subpopulation in the inner regions is consistent with stellar data from Spolaor and Proctor showing a decreasing profile up to 10 . Conversely, the velocity dispersion of the blue GCs increases with radius. NGC 2768 NGC 2768 is classified as E 6 in Vaucouleurs and as S 0_ 1/2 in Sandage . Spectroscopic studies of the very central regions McDermid have also suggested a young ( 2.5 Gyr) stellar population associated with the disk, as supported by the recent supernova SN2000ds Filippenko . Kundu studied the HST /WFPC2 photometry for 113 GC candidates in this galaxy, finding a statistically significant probability of it having a bimodal colour distribution. In this work, NGC 2768 was imaged with R_ C iz filters, in good seeing conditions but with the lowest exposure time among our sample of galaxies. Using KMM we found the colour distribution to be bimodal, with the blue and red peaks at (R_ C - z) = 0.41 0.01 and 0.70 0.02 respectively. Bimodality was also found in the HST /ACS West pointing used to design the DEIMOS masks in the central region. The colour peaks occur at (F435W F814W) =1.77 0.03 and 2.17 0.01 respectively. We find 109 spectroscopically confirmed GCs over 5 DEIMOS masks observed in sub-arcsec seeing conditions. Using a colour split at (R_ C z)=0.57 and excluding the marginal GCs, we investigate the kinematics of the resulting 60 blue and 42 red GCs respectively. We find significant rotation only for the red GCs, which rotate roughly along the photometric major axis in agreement with the host galaxy stars. The 2 -D velocity field of the red GCs is akin to that of ATLAS ^ 3D in the inner regions (Figure fig:2D ). The blue GCs have a marginally higher velocity dispersion profile than the red GCs. NGC 3377 NGC 3377 is a classical E6 elliptical in the Leo I group and the closest galaxy in our sample. The resolved stellar properties of NGC 3377 have been studied by Harris07A that found no evidence for any young ( < 3 Gyr) stellar population. Coccato studied 159 PNe in this galaxy, detecting significant rotation within 2 arcmin and a kinematic major axis twist of 80 deg from the major towards the minor axis. Kundu , Chies-Santos and Cho studied the GC system of this galaxy using HST imaging, finding the colour distribution to be likely bimodal. NGC 3377 was imaged with gri filters and gz filters for ground based and space based observations respectively. We found a high probability for the colour distribution to be bimodal, both in our Suprime-Cam and in the supplementary ACS archive imaging, as already found in Chies-Santos . In this work, we present radial velocities for 126 GCs, observed in 4 DEIMOS masks. It is worth noting that we extend the kinematics of this galaxy to 8 \ 25 kpc, two times further than the PNe studies. We rule out two GCs that are likely to belong to the spiral galaxy NGC 3377 A (V _ sys 573 ) that lies 8 arcmin North-West from NGC 3377 . With a colour split at (g-i)=0.88 we study the kinematics of 57 blue and 60 red GCs, respectively. We detect significant rotation for the red GC subpopulation along the photometric major axis in agreement with PNe and long--slit data Coccato .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3377 as an aligned E5-6 galaxy with an embedded disk, companion, declining stellar and PNe rotation, and blue globular clusters with little rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3377 is a bright elliptical galaxy of E5-6 morphological type Sandage1984 , characterized by a small disk embedded in a boxy halo Halliday2001 . This galaxy is a member of the Leo I group with the smaller NGC 3377A being its nearest companion galaxy. The GC system contains 126 objects, characterized by a bimodal colour distribution as was already found by Pota2013 .

NGC 3377 shows roughly constant ( 80 100 kms^ 1 ) stellar rotation velocity out to 1 R_ e , followed by a subsequent decrease out to 2 2.5 R_ e . ATLAS ^ 3D has shown that the stellar rotation velocity is also high in the central 0.5 R_ e , consistent with the SLUGGS stellar kinematics. The PNe also show a decreasing rotation velocity profile out to 2 3 R_ e , which is overall consistent with that of the stars within 1 2 R_ e despite an offset of 40 50 kms^ 1 at 1 R_ e . At larger radii, i.e. beyond 3 R_ e , the rotation velocity of the PNe shows evidence of slightly increasing again towards North (see Fig. A2), where the rotation, possibly, occurs along a different PA _ kin than that of the inner 2 R_ e (see Fig. 3). The red GCs show decreasing rotation velocity profile out to 3 R_ e , which is consistent with both that of the stars and PNe in the overlapping radii.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3377 halo imaging as a crowding-rich testbed, describing its Leo-group early-type nature, RGB halo population, and NN-modeled completeness behavior.

quotes.quote: A useful example of a single field that displays a larger range of crowding is the HST ACS deep imaging of the halo of NGC 3377. This galaxy is an intermediate-sized ETG in the Leo group at d=11 Mpc, at high Galactic latitude ( b=58^o ) and low foreground extinction ( A_V = 0.09 ). The raw HST images comprise total exposures of 38500 sec in F606W and 22260 sec in F814W and were originally used to measure the metallicity distribution of the resolved red-giant-branch (RGB) stars in the halo harris+2007 . Since NGC 3377 is not a giant galaxy, its scale size is small enough ( R_ eff 2 kpc 38'' ) that the degree of crowding changes strongly across the ACS field of view moving outward from the galaxy center. A 3-kpc portion of the field is shown in Figure fig:n3377field where the gradient of surface brightness is clearly visible see also Figures 1 and 2 of harris+2007 .

Our new DOLPHOT reductions for the NGC 3377 field are shown in Figure fig:cmd_4panel . At all galactocentric radii the CMD of the recovered objects is completely dominated by many thousands of halo RGB (red-giant-branch) stars with negligible field contamination. In all radial zones, the sharp lower boundary of the CMD is due to the SNR limit imposed in the selection stage. To set up the data for the subsequent modelling, a set of 5 10^5 artificial stars distributed uniformly across the field was used, covering the magnitude range F475W = 23 to 36 and color range F606W F814W = -1 to 5 , and following an exponentially rising luminosity function with a=0.7 (Eq. \ ( eq:lf )) to match a normal old RGB LF. An important difference between the NGC 3377 and NGC 1275 datasets is that for NGC 3377, the RGB star population itself is the dominant source of the local sky noise. Separate parameters for sky noise and crowding are thus not needed and we use only the crowding parameter to represent both.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3377 has an S-bend velocity profile and poor spherical fits, with only slight dark-halo improvement on one axis before omitting it from conclusions.

quotes.quote: NGC 3377 Stellar data for this flattened (E5) galaxy show a pronounced ``S-bend'' in the _ los profile. All the fits are formally bad. On the first axis, a halo with high F_ d brings a slight -- but perhaps insignificant -- improvement. On the second axis, a dark halo does not improve the fit at all, and the F_ d =9.5 and 9.9 curves are worse than the others. We suspect that complicated orbital structure (especially the undulating projected kinematics) renders straightforward spherical models inadequate and inappliable to NGC 3377.

Details of this profile have been attributed to the influence of a central massive object and discy dynamics gebhardt2003,copin04 . coccato2009 note a twist in the PN kinematics. We omit NGC 3377 from consideration in our conclusions.

sources.summary: The paper reports a higher but consistent black-hole mass for NGC 3377, cites earlier detections, and describes it as a rapid rotator with a nuclear X-ray jet source.

quotes.quote: NGC 3377. The black hole mass for this galaxy increased by 70 , compared to G03, mainly caused by the stronger widening of the confidence contours at the high-mass end than at the low-mass end. The previous value is still fully consistent within 1 . Copin:2004 reported a black hole mass of M_ =8.3 10^7 M_ (for our assumed distance) based on Integral Field Unit (IFU) observations with SAURON and OASIS, also still consistent with our results within 1 . The first detection of a black hole in NGC 3377 has been reported by Kormendy:1998 , based on ground-based observations. Using an isotropic model, they found M_ =2.1 10^8 M_ and M/L_V =2.0 (for our assumed distance), in good agreement with our results.
NGC 3377 is a rapid rotator and close to isotropy, justifying the isotropic assumption for this galaxy. NGC 3377 exhibits a nuclear X-ray source, showing a jet like feature Soria:2006 .

sources.summary: The paper finds an unexpected faint globular-cluster excess in NGC 3377, examines whether the objects are contaminants or diffuse clusters, and notes a possible red-cluster selection bias.

quotes.quote: As can be seen in Figure fig:gclf_g , there is an excess of faint GCs around g 25.5 mag in the GCLF of NGC 3377. These faint GCs deviate significantly from the fitted Gaussian function. This deviation is also found at z 24.5 mag in the z band GCLF shown in Figure fig:gclf_z .

In Figure fig:n3377star , the spatial distribution, size, and colour of NGC 3377 GCs fainter than g=25 mag are plotted along with those brighter than g=25 mag. In the top-left panel, the faint GCs (large closed circles) appear to be uniformly distributed, unlike the bright GCs (small open circles) which are concentrated at the galaxy center.

However, unlike the other GC systems in our sample, the mean colour of the NGC 3377 GC system lies in the middle of the range of ACSVCS mean colours. Because the distribution of red GCs is more centrally concentrated and NGC 3377 is the nearest galaxy in our sample, it is possible that the GCs detected within our ACS field are biased toward red GCs.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3377 as an E5/6 SLUGGS galaxy with an embedded disc, broad globular-cluster age and metallicity distributions, and the youngest median GC age among the sample.

quotes.quote: We apply our MCMC age--metallicity technique to GCs around three galaxies from the SLUGGS survey - NGC 1407, NGC 3115 and NGC 3377. NGC 1407 is a slowly rotating E0 galaxy with a stellar mass of 4 10^ 11 M _ , NGC 3115 is a fast rotating S0 galaxy with a stellar mass of 9 10^ 10 M _ , and NGC 3377 is an E5/6 galaxy with an embedded disc and a stellar mass of 3 10^ 10 M _ 2014ApJ...791...80A, 2017MNRAS.464.4611F . For the GCs in these three galaxies we use Subaru Suprime-Cam gri photometry presented in 2013MNRAS.428..389P for NGC 1407 and NGC 3377 and in 2011ApJ...736L..26A for NGC 3115 and assume an uncertainty in the reddening of E(B - V) = 0.01 mag for these GCs.

The four galaxies show wildly different GC age--metallicity distributions. NGC 1407 shows both an old unimodal age distribution (with a possible tail to intermediate ages) and a unimodal metallicity distribution. NGC 3115 shows broad age and metallicity distributions with a clear separation between a metal poor age--metallicity sequence and a metal rich sequence. NGC 3377 also shows broad age and metallicity distributions with a clear age--metallicity sequence.

The median ages of the different galaxy populations and metallicity subpopulations reinforce the diversity of age distributions. NGC 3377 has the youngest median age while NGC 1407 and the MW have the oldest.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3377 as a disky low-luminosity elliptical with declining rotation, noting data-set discrepancies and cautioning that low-mass galaxy rotation measures can be systematically affected.

quotes.quote: NGC 3377 --Disky, low-luminosity elliptical in a loose group (Leo I). The kinematic data are from SAURON inside 0.7 , deep long-slit VLT/FORS2 major axis spectra to 3.4 \ 2009MNRAS.394.1249C , and 112 PN velocities between 1 and 12 \ 2009MNRAS.394.1249C . In comparing data-sets in their overlap regions (including other long-slit kinematics from the literature: Bender et al. 1994; Kormendy et al. 1998; Halliday et al. 2001; Prugniel Simien 2002) we discovered some fairly large discrepancies, particularly in the velocity dispersion.

Assuming that the higher spectral resolution of the FORS2 results makes them more reliable, we therefore subtract 59 \ in quadrature from the SAURON dispersions, and multiply the SAURON rotation velocities by a factor of 1.13. The impact of this issue on rotation velocity and \ is not large, but we note that characterizations of low-mass galaxies in general using quantities such as v_ rot / _ p should be viewed with caution since they could be strongly affected by systematic problems, as in NGC 3377. There is some indication of a 20^ kinematic twist in the outer parts of this galaxy, where the true rotation velocity could be 60 \ rather than 20 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3377 as a moderately bright elliptical, combining stellar and halo tracers in Jeans analysis to infer an intermediate-mass dark halo.

quotes.quote: The mass profile for the moderately bright elliptical NGC 3377 is studied through an spherical Jeans analysis, combined with a Bayesian approach. The prior distributions are generated from dark matter simulations and observational constraints. The observational data set consist of Gemini/GMOS long-slit observations aligned with the major and minor axes of the galaxy, and are supplemented with data from the literature for the diffuse stellar component, globular clusters and planetary nebulae.

Although the galaxy is assumed to alternatively reside in central and satellite haloes, the comparison with literature results prefer the latter option. Several options of constant anisotropy are considered, as well as both NFW and Einasto mass profiles. The analysis points to an intermediate mass halo, presenting a virial mass around (3.6 0.6) 10^ 11 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3377 nuclear X-ray emission, describing a jet-like elongated feature, no obvious optical isophote distortion, and no corresponding stellar residual feature.

quotes.quote: In the remaining three galaxies, NGC 3377, NGC 5845 and NGC 821, structure in the nuclear region is more clearly visible. In the other two galaxies, NGC 821 and NGC 3377, the emission has instead an elongated, ``jet-like'' appearance. In NGC 3377 (Figure 1, top left), an elongated feature seems to link the nuclear source with another, fainter source located 3 160 pc to the East.

For NGC 3377, we compared the Chandra image with an archival HST /WFPC2 image in the F555W filter, to determine whether the elongated feature in the X-ray image has an optical counterpart; however, we do not see any obvious distortions in the optical isophotes (Figure 7, top panel). We then searched for possible fainter optical signatures in the nuclear region of NGC 3377. The result for NGC 3377 derived in this manner is shown in the bottom panel of Figure 7: we find a small, brighter stellar disk but no feature corresponding to the X-ray elongation, neither parallel not perpendicular to the disk plane.

sources.summary: NGC 3377 is described as an elongated elliptical whose kinematic map indicates an embedded disk, dusty fast-rotating inner regions, and boxier, slower outer regions.

quotes.quote: The combined stellar kinematic map of this elongated elliptical (E5-6) galaxy presents several key characteristics of an embedded disk within a more spheroidal component. The inner regions are dusty, have disky isophotes (e.g. Bender:1988vj ), rotate rapidly, and have depressed velocity dispersions compared to larger radii. The outer regions are boxier and rotate more slowly but remain aligned with the inner regions within about 4 \ (see also Coccato:2009je ). Pota:2013en reported significant rotation along the major axis with outer twist of the metal-rich GCs in agreement with stars and long-slit data. The metal-poor GCs do not show significant rotation.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3377's inner photometry is poor, yet its colour/CaT metallicity disagreement persists; its colour-metallicity relation is steep and CaT metallicities are bimodal.

quotes.quote: The inner regions of NGC 3377 also suffer from poor photometry; however when GCs with poor photometry are removed, the disagreement between colour and CaT-based metallicity remains.

Both NGC 3377 and NGC 4494 show the opposite trend, with agreement at low metallicities and the colour-based metallicities giving lower values than the CaT-based ones at high metallicities (Figures fig:zhist f, i , fig:metalmetal f, i ). In both NGC 3377 and NGC 4494 the relationship between colour and metallicity appears to be linear and to be steeper than in other galaxies.

Using GMM (Section GMM , Table tab:gmm-metal , Figure fig:metalmetal ), we detected CaT metallicity bimodality with a probability of greater than 0.95 in NGC 3115, NGC 4278 and NGC 4365 and greater than 0.90 in NGC 1407, NGC 5846 and NGC 3377.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC3377, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC3377 (Fig. fig:NGC3377 ) is classified as E5 in the RC3 catalogue. Yet, the surface-brightness profile exhibits an exponential decline at radii >60 , and closer inspection of the and B_4 profiles reveal peaks at R 8 and R 30 , respectively, suggesting two embedded disks. A single- \ model not only produces quadrupole residuals, but also a high \ index of 8.7.

sources.summary: The appendix describes NGC 3377 as having a very blue depleted core, disk-component light-profile fit, dust contamination, and an outward-reddening color gradient.

quotes.quote: NGC 3377 NGC 3377 has an extremely depleted core, which is also very blue. Excluding the central region, we can fit the light profile of this galaxy with a S e rsic profile plus an outer disk component. NGC 3377 has a dust lane as well as diffuse dust contamination in the g band. There is a steep color gradient from the nucleus to the outer regions of the galaxy, with the g-z color getting redder with increasing radius.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3377 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: It is an E6 galaxy in the Leo I group. The absorption line kinematics 2009MNRAS.394.1249C,2004MNRAS.352..721E,2016MNRAS.457..147F shows that the galaxy displays a disk-like rotation in the inner regions, which starts to decrease at 1.5 . Our analysis of the PN kinematics shows that the decreasing trend continues out to large radii, where it becomes 16 8 at 4 and 11 7 beyond 7 . The kinematic major axis position angle twists with radius in the outskirts in agreement with 2016MNRAS.457..147F .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3377 globular clusters have stronger red-color CaT values than predicted, bluer low- and high-CaT colors, and higher low-CaT Na82 strengths.

quotes.quote: At blue colours the GCs of NGC 1407 and NGC 4278 have weaker CaT values than predicted by Equation eq:CaT_colour . For red colours, NGC 1407, NGC 4278, NGC 4365 and NGC 4649 GCs have weaker CaT strengths than predicted from their colours while NGC 3115, NGC 3377, NGC 4473 and NGC 4494 have stronger than predicted CaT values. The shapes of the colour--CaT relations of NGC 1407 and NGC 4494 are radically different as first noticed by 2011MNRAS.415.3393F . As seen in Figure fig:stacking_CaT1 , at low CaT values NGC 3115, NGC 3377 and NGC 4473 show bluer colours compared to the other galaxies while at high CaT values NGC 3377 and NGC 4494 also show bluer colours than the other galaxies. Additionally, at low CaT values NGC 3115, NGC 3377 and NGC 4473 show higher Na82 strengths than the other galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 3377's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 3377: For this E6 galaxy we use a single profile, which gives R_e=39.1'' , roughly consistent with Arnold2014 . The multi-component fit of Laesker2014 delivers R_e=7'' , but their disk component is faint and the envelope could well be part of the bulge. The M/L is scaled to the distance of KormendyHo2013 and from I to R- band using the (R-I) color from Hyperleda.

gmw_00000589.name: NGC 1407
gmw_00000589.overview: NGC 1407 is a massive, nearly circular giant elliptical galaxy that dominates the Eridanus A group [arXiv:0908.3705, arXiv:1203.2578]. It hosts a weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1209.4351] and exhibits restarted radio activity, featuring an inner double radio source surrounded by older, diffuse emission [arXiv:1206.5751].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1407 as a giant early-type galaxy, comparing CaT metallicities with previous gradients and finding limited radial coverage but reasonable agreement.

quotes.quote: NGC 1407 NGC 1407 is the brightest group galaxy that dominates the Eridanus A group (Brough et al. 2006). It is a giant elliptical galaxy with a clear core-like luminosity profile (Spolaor et al. 2008a). It has been measured to have a uniform old age with a steep metallicity gradient and high element ratio within one effective radius (S08b). In their study of the stellar kinematics, Spolaor et al. (2008a) found tentative signs of a kinematically decoupled core (KDC) at the centre of NGC 1407.

NGC 1407 This galaxy is the only galaxy in our sample for which we have previous literature gradient values available to compare our results to. Unfortunately, it is not our best dataset in that the radial coverage is limited. Nevertheless, as seen in Fig. fig:NGC1407 , the general agreement of our CaT measured metallicities with the metallicities measured from long-slit observations by S08b is reasonable. Although the individual values agree with the S08b data within the errors at similar galactocentric radii, the rolling average shows a slight offset in metallicity ( 0.3 dex).

sources.summary: The survey presents NGC 1407 as a massive Eridanus A elliptical with an old stellar population, massive dark halo, rich GC sample, and rotating blue and red GC subpopulations.

quotes.quote: NGC 1407 is a massive E 0 galaxy at the centre of the dwarf galaxy dominated Eridanus A group Brough . It shows moderate rotation along the photometric major axis and has a weak central AGN Zhang . The stellar population analysis of SpolaorB found the galaxy to possess a uniformly old age within 0.6 . A dynamical analysis of NGC 1407 was given by Romanowsky09 . They used Suprime-Cam imaging and DEIMOS spectra for 172 GCs and found a massive dark halo.

We supplement the spectroscopic sample of Romanowsky09 with 6 additional DEIMOS masks, that make NGC 1407 , with a total of 369 spectroscopically confirmed GCs, the most populous spectro-photometric dataset in our galaxy sample. We detect rotation along the photometric major axis for both GC subpopulations. The blue GCs rotate in the innermost radial bin along the photometric minor axis and between 3 and 5 \ along the photometric major axis.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1407 as a massive elliptical, using stars and globular clusters to constrain its dark matter profile, IMF, virial mass, stellar mass, and dark-matter-dominated halo.

quotes.quote: We perform in-depth dynamical modelling of the luminous and dark matter (DM) content of the elliptical galaxy NGC 1407. Our strategy consists of solving the spherical Jeans equations for three independent dynamical tracers: stars, blue GCs and red GCs in a self-consistent manner. We adopt a maximum-likelihood Markov-Chain Monte Carlo fitting technique in the attempt to constrain the inner slope of the DM density profile (the cusp/core problem), and the stellar initial mass function (IMF) of the galaxy. We find the inner logarithmic slope of the DM density profiles to be = 0.6 0.4 , which is consistent with either a DM cusp ( = 1 ) or with a DM core ( = 0) .

The virial mass of NGC 1407 is M_ vir = 13.3 0.2 M_ , whereas the stellar mass is M_* = 11.8 0.1 M_ . The overall uncertainties on the mass of NGC 1407 are only 5 per cent at the projected stellar effective radius. We attribute the disagreement between our results and previous X-ray results to the gas not being in hydrostatic equilibrium in the central regions of the galaxy. The halo of NGC 1407 is found be DM dominated, with a dynamical mass-to-light ratio of M/L=260_ 100 ^ +174 M_ /L_ , B .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1407's globular clusters to test nonlinear CaT and color-metallicity relations, noting bimodal model distributions but an unreproduced metal-poor CaT bump.

quotes.quote: We also perform the projection simulations for GC systems of individual ETGs. Middle and bottom rows in Figure f8 show the large ETG NGC 1407 observed by 2012MNRAS.426.1475U and 2010AJ....139.1566F . 2010AJ....139.1566F observed CaT of 144 GCs in NGC 1407, and 2012MNRAS.426.1475U added more GCs and remeasured CaT for all GCs.

The observation by 2010AJ....139.1566F shows that only the (B-I)_0 distribution has bimodality but the CaT distribution prefers skewed Gaussian distribution. Our projection test based on 13 Gyr relations reproduces simultaneously both the CaT and (B-I)_0 observations of 2010AJ....139.1566F using the Gaussian MDF of Fe/H =-0.80 with _ Fe/H =0.55 . On the other hand, the GCs of 2012MNRAS.426.1475U show bimodality for both the (g-i)_0 and CaT distributions. Our models for NGC 1407 show bimodal distributions both in (g-i)_0 and CaT . However, the metal-poor bump (at CaT_ F10 4.8 ) in the CaT distribution is not reproduced.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1407 as a massive early-type brightest-group galaxy, confirming old stellar populations, a steep metallicity gradient, and no anomalous outer metallicity peaks.

quotes.quote: Located at the centre of the massive Eridanus group ( Gould1993 ), NGC 1407 is the brightest group galaxy, classified as an E0. Its stellar mass is log(M _ /M _ ) 11.60 Forbes2017 ) and its total halo mass is log(M _h /M _ ) 13.2 Wasserman2018 . Here we take the distance to NGC 1407 (26.8 Mpc) and its effective radius (R _e = 63.4") from P+14 P+14 to enable direct comparison. The galaxy has been extensively studied both with classical long-slit spectroscopy and with IFS. It has a recession velocity of v_ =1779 km s ^ 1 and shows a well defined kinematically distinct core (KDC).

While NGC 1407 was previously found to have stellar populations characteristic of massive ETGs (with radially constant old ages and high alpha-enhancements), it was claimed to show peculiar super-solar metallicity peaks at large radius that deviated from an otherwise strong negative metallicity gradient, which is hard to reconcile within a `two-phase' formation scenario. Our outer pointing confirms the near-uniform old ages and the presence of a steep metallicity gradient, but with no evidence for anomalously high metallicity values at large galactocentric radii. We find a rising outer velocity dispersion profile and high values of the 4th-order kinematic moment -- an indicator of possible anisotropy.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1407's globular cluster system, finding a clear bimodal color distribution, broad subpopulations, different blue and red core radii, and GC structure similar to the galaxy light.

quotes.quote: We present wide-field Keck telescope imaging of the globular cluster (GC) systems around NGC 1407 and NGC 1400 in the Eridanus galaxy cloud. This is complemented by Hubble Space Telescope images from the Advanced Camera for Surveys of NGC 1407 and Wide Field and Planetary Camera 2 images of NGC 1400. We clearly detect bimodality in the GC colour distribution of NGC 1407. The blue GC subpopulation has a mean colour of B-I = 1.61 and a relative contribution of around 40 , whereas the red subpopulation with B-I = 2.06 contributes 60 to the overall GC system. Assuming old ages, this corresponds to [ Fe/H ] = --1.45 and --0.19.

We fit isothermal-like profiles and derive GC system core radii of 9.4 kpc for NGC 1407 and 5.8 kpc for NGC 1400. For NGC 1407 we are able to separate the surface density distribution into blue and red subpopulations, giving 17.8 and 7.6 kpc respectively. Outside this central region, the radial profile of the GC surface density is similar to that of the galaxy light for NGC 1407 but it is flatter for NGC 1400.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1407 and its group with X-ray and optical data, finding a hot-gas transition, a flattened halo-boundary feature, and a very dark group halo.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the mass distributions in the bright E0 galaxy NGC 1407 and its associated group by analyzing the high quality  and  X-ray spectroscopic data. In order to probe the stellar mass distribution we calculated the B-band mass-to-light ratio profile by comparing the observed line-strength indices and multi-color photometric data with different stellar synthesis model predictions. Based on the recent survey results we also have modeled the contribution from other group members to the stellar mass. We find that the gas is single-phase with a temperature of 0.7 keV within 1  (1  = 9.0 kpc), which is typical for elliptical galaxies. Outside 1  the gas temperature increases quickly outwards to >1 keV, indicating its group origin.

With the high spatial resolution of  we reveal that the X-ray surface brightness profile shows a central excess in the innermost region, and on both the total mass and dark matter profiles there is a flattened feature at about ^ < _ 1 , which coincides with the gas temperature transition from the galactic level to the group level. We speculate that this may be a mark of the boundary between the galaxy and group halos, as has been seen in some other cluster/group-dominating galaxies.

At the virial radius r_ 200 =572 118 kpc, the inferred mass and mass-to-light ratio are M_ 200 =2.20 0.42 10^ 13  and M_ vir /L_ B =311 60 , respectively, showing that the NGC 1407 group is an extremely dark system even comparable to many clusters of galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1407 as the dominant early-type galaxy of its group, measuring kinematics, photometric structure, and a possible central black-hole mass while finding no interaction signature.

quotes.quote: This is the first paper of a series focused on investigating the star formation and evolutionary history of the two early-type galaxies NGC 1407 and NGC 1400. They are the two brightest galaxies of the NGC 1407 (or Eridanus-A) group, one of the 60 groups studied as part of the Group Evolution Multi-wavelength Study (GEMS). Here we present new high signal-to-noise long-slit spectroscopic data obtained at the ESO 3.6m telescope and high-resolution multi-band imaging data from the HST/ACS and wide-field imaging from Subaru Suprime-Cam. We spatially resolved integrated spectra out to 0.6 (NGC 1407) and 1.3 (NGC 1400) effective radii.
The NGC 1407 group is dominated by the NGC 1407 galaxy.

The early-type galaxies NGC 1407 and NGC 1400 (see Table ga_prop for details) lie at small projected angular distance on the sky ( 12 arcmin) but with a remarkable difference in velocity, v 1100 km s ^ 1 . NGC 1407 is the brightest group galaxy, whereas NGC 1400 is the second brightest galaxy with a peculiar velocity of 558 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1407 as a group-central elliptical whose X-ray circular velocity curve rises steeply and remains discrepant from dynamical models.

quotes.quote: NGC 1399 is located at the centre of the Fornax cluster, NGC 1407 and NGC 5846 at the centre of groups, and NGC 4472, NGC 4486 and NGC 4649 at the centre of sub-clumps in the irregular Virgo cluster. NGC 4486 is at the centre of the most massive sub-component. determined the velocity dispersion of the environment surrounding NGC 1399 from 92 galaxies within a radius of about 2.7 (930 Mpc), from 35 galaxies within about 900 kpc of NGC 1407 and from 87 galaxies within a radius of 1.8 (777 kpc) of NGC 5846. calculated the velocity dispersion in sub-clumps containing NGC 4472, NGC 4486 and NGC 4649 using 166, 62 and 58 galaxies respectively.
