Recently the H.E.S.S. collaboration has presented evidence for a ray signal at the 4 level (up to 5.3 using a low energy threshold analysis) above 200 GeV corresponding to about 2 of the Crab flux . PG 1553+113 was observed with the MAGIC telescope in 2005 at about the same time as the H.E.S.S. observations took place.

Combining the data from 2005 and 2006, a very clear signal was detected with a total significance of 8.8 . In rays there was no evidence for short term variability on a time scale of days, but a significant change by a factor of three in the flux level from 2005 to 2006 was found. The combined 2005 and 2006 differential energy spectrum for PG 1553+113 is well described by a pure power law with a photon index =4.2 0.4 . The signal detected by MAGIC confirms the tentative signal seen by H.E.S.S. at a higher energy threshold with data taken at about the same time as MAGIC in the 2005 period .

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1553+113 as a high-frequency-peaked BL Lac with unknown redshift, constraining its VHE spectrum and possible jet-component break after EBL correction.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113 was discovered in the Palomar-Green survey of UV-excess stellar objects green . It is classified as a BL Lac object based on its featureless spectrum redshift1, redshift2 and its significant optical variability variability . PG 1553+113 is well studied from the radio to the X-ray regime. Based on its broad-band spectral energy distribution (SED), PG 1553+113 is now classified as a high-frequency peaked BL Lac classification , similar to most of the AGNs detected at very high energies (VHE).

Here we correct the combined H.E.S.S and MAGIC spectrum of PG 1553+113 for this absorption assuming a minimum density of the evolving EBL. We use an argument that the intrinsic photon index cannot be harder than = 1.5 and derive an upper limit on the redshift of z < 0.69 .

Alternatively, this break might be attributed to an additional emission component in the jet of PG 1553+113. This would be the first evidence for a second component is detected in the VHE spectrum of a blazar.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1555+1111 as a distant TeV HBL with a bent parsec-scale jet, confirmed INOV, longer-term brightening, and a notable optical dip.

quotes.quote: J1555+1111 (PG 1553 + 113, z=0.360 ) This most distant HBL with a firm TeV detection (Abdo et al. 2009) is known to show a highly time dependent variability behaviour at different frequencies. Its radio structure is marked by an extremely large bend (by 110^ ) of the parsec-scale jet towards the outer lobe (Rector, Gabuzda Stocke 2003). It showed INOV on one of the two nights it was monitored during mid-1999 as part of our INOV programme (Stalin et al. \ 2005).

Those data have been included in our computation of the INOV duty cycle (Table 3). In our monitoring, confirmed variability was detected on all 3 nights both using C_ eff and F statistics. In the longer term, a clear brightening by 0.25 mag was observed between the first two epochs of our monitoring, which were separated by almost a year.
A clear case of internight variability also occured when the source brightened by another 0.04 mag between 15 and 16 May 2010 (Fig. \ 3).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes MAGIC campaign data for blazar PG 1553+113, adopting z=0.4 and finding both EBL-only and EBL-plus-ALP curves fit the observations.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113 This blazar was first detected in the VHE regime by the HESS collaboration in 2005 . Since then, it has been extensively observed by the MAGIC collaboration, whose results are detailed in ref. . In our study, the combined VHE data from this five-year campaign by MAGIC are used. The SSC modeling is also taken from ref. .

For the redshift of the source we assume z=0.4 , warning the reader about its uncertainty. The result from applying our ALP formalism to this blazar is shown in figure fig:blazars (lower-right panel). In this case, as in the PKS 1222+216 case, both the EBL-only and EBL-plus-ALPs curves provide a good fit to the observed data.

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1553+113 as a bright TeV blazar with an uncertain redshift, a compact 22 GHz VLBA core, a short northeastern jet, and polarized core emission.

quotes.quote: We observed the five TeV blazars 1ES 1101-232, Markarian 180, 1ES 1218+304, PG 1553+113, and H 2356-309 at five epochs each from 2006 to 2009 with the VLBA under observation code BP146, at frequencies from 5 to 22 GHz, for a total of 25 new VLBA images produced for this paper.
Polarization observations were made of Markarian 180 and PG 1553+113 (the two brightest sources) at the first epoch.

PG 1553+113 The redshift of this source is somewhat uncertain. The redshift has been constrained to be in the range from 0.43 to 0.58 , and we have used the mean of this range, or z =0.5, for results requiring a redshift measurement. This source is relatively bright, and our VLBA observations were made at a frequency of 22 GHz. This source is quite compact at 22 GHz, with a 100 mJy core and a short jet to the northeast that can be modeled as a single 25 mJy component about 0.2 mas from the core. A single epoch of polarization observations at the first epoch showed the core to be 3 polarized, with the electric vector position angle aligned with the jet position angle.

sources.summary: The paper studies a 2012 flare of the BL Lac PG 1553+113, measuring VHE spectral curvature, redshift constraints near z=0.4, and multiwavelength SSC behavior.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113 is a very-high-energy (VHE, E>100 GeV ) ray emitter classified as a BL Lac object. Its redshift is constrained by intergalactic absorption lines in the range 0.4<z<0.58 . The MAGIC telescopes have monitored the source's activity since 2005.

The VHE spectrum shows clear curvature, being well fitted either by a power law with an exponential cut-off or by a log-parabola. A simple power-law fit hypothesis for the observed shape of the PG 1553+113 VHE ray spectrum is rejected with a high significance (fit probability P=2.6 10^ 6 ). The observed curvature is compatible with the extragalactic background light (EBL) imprint predicted by current generation EBL models assuming a redshift z 0.4 . New constraints on the redshift are derived from the VHE spectrum.

sources.summary: PG 1553+113 is treated as a similar luminous high-frequency blazar with uncertain redshift; earlier one-zone SSC models depend strongly on assumed distance.

quotes.quote: The SED of \ is indeed very similar to the SED of PG 1553+113, and both sources can be seen as examples of luminous and high-frequency peaked blazars. However, it is important to remind the reader that the exact redshift of PG 1553+113 is still uncertain, and the current best estimates constrain it to be between 0.395 and 0.58 Danforth10 . The value of z=0.5 adopted here is compatible with the observational constraints, but the source may be closer, and thus less luminous than what is shown in Fig. figasdc .

Before presenting in details the modeling of , it is interesting to discuss the case of PG 1553+113 which, as presented above, shows a SED similar to the one from . Different authors have successfully modeled its emission using a simple one-zone SSC model Albert07, Abdo10, Aleksic10, Tavecchio10, Zhang12 . However, the uncertainty in the distance of this object complicates the modeling task and indeed most solutions adopted redshift values which have later been proven to be incorrect. Albert07 successfully modeled the SED assuming z=0.3, and found that for z>0.56 the emission from PG 1553+113 is not compatible with a simple one-zone SSC model.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PG 1553+113 as an HBL BL Lac object with uncertain redshift and very-high-energy emission, then reports an optical outburst.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113 Located in the Serpens Caput constellation, PG 1553+113 was classified as a BL Lac object based on its featureless spectrum 1990PASP..102.1120F and was classified as a HBL blazar 1994ApJS...93..125F,1995A&AS..109..267G,2002A&A...383..410B . Evidence of very-high-energy ray emission from this source was first reported by H.E.S.S. in 2005 2006A&A...448L..19A and was later confirmed by observations above 200 GeV with the MAGIC telescope at a significance level of 8.8 2007ApJ...654L.119A . Due to its featureless optical spectrum, the redshift of PG 1553+113 remains highly uncertain. Measurements using the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) Cosmic Origins Spectrograph (COS) yielded a lower limit of 0.395 2010ApJ...720..976D . The small statistical uncertainties of the VERITAS energy spectrum provided a robust upper limit of 0.62 2015ApJ...799....7A .

We observed this source on 28 nights in the , i , and o bands between 2009 May 26 and 2010 March 28. One can see from Figure fig:all that there is an outburst of about 0.2 mags on a timescale of 23 days (from JD 2454988 to JD 2455011).

sources.summary: The paper adopts PG 1553+113 as the BL Lac template for mock CTA observations, using its sky position, redshift lower limit, VHE detections, flare history, and intrinsic blazar spectrum.

quotes.quote: Therefore, we will assume an observation of the BL Lac object PG 1553+113 located at a sky position The position of the source in the sky determines the reconversion probability in the Galactic magnetic field . of _ J2000 = 15^ h 55^ m 43.0^ s and _ J2000 =+11^ d 11^ m 24.3^ s . A lower limit on the redshift of z 0.4 has been inferred from Ly- absorption lines in the optical spectrum . The source has been observed in the VHE regime with H.E.S.S. , MAGIC , and VERITAS , and it underwent a flaring episode in 2012 where its integrated flux above 100 GeV reached the level of the Crab nebula . To illustrate our method, we will make the assumption that this source is located in a galaxy cluster when considering the ICM scenarios. The 2005 H.E.S.S. observation of PG 1553+113 serves as a template for the intrinsic blazar spectrum.

With the suggested method, modifications of the spectra should be detectable for couplings g_ 11 2 and ALP masses m_ neV 100 for a viable range of the ambient magnetic field strength given a 20 hours observation of a flaring AGN with properties similar to the object PG 1553+113 with an intrinsic spectrum that follows a power-law extrapolation up to 7.4 TeV (corresponding to = 12 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1553+113 as a variable high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac, simulating a flaring CTA observation and cluster-field photon-ALP mixing scenario.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113 The variable high synchrotron peaked BL Lac (HBL) has been observed with H.E.S.S. for 7.6 hours in 2005 and 17.2 hours in 2006 , with MAGIC for 9.5 hours in 2006 and 7.2 hours in 2008 , and with VERITAS for 50 hours between 2010 to 2011 . Using the lower limit z 0.4 , the H.E.S.S. observations extend to an optical depth 3.95 for the highest energy data point at 0.949 TeV. In 2012, MAGIC observed the source in a flaring state lasting for several days reaching up to 100 of the Crab nebula flux above 100 GeV in an observation campaign spanning a total of 17.4 hours . We will use the H.E.S.S. observation of the source since the MAGIC observations do not extend to 4 .

A cross correlation with the galaxy cluster catalogs reveals that the source is within 1.39 Mpc of a poor cluster with 8 member galaxies and ID GMBCG 587742629068931733. The redshift difference is z = 0.035 , assuming z = 0.4 .

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1553+113 as the most distant sample blazar, fits a hybrid SED, and finds SSC emission dominates while neutrino flux remains below the gamma-ray peak.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113 at z=0.4 aleksic12 . This is not only the most distant source of this sample, but also belongs to the class of most distant TeV blazars in general. Its redshift has not been firmly measured, yet the value adopted here can be considered as a safe estimate (for different techniques and results, see aleksic12 ). Similarly to 1ES 1011+496 below, it has been only recently detected in VHE rays by both H.E.S.S. aharonian06 and MAGIC albert07b .

PG 1553+113 The hybrid SED of blazar PG 1553+113 is shown in Fig. pg1553+113 . The contribution of different emission processes to the total SED is depicted with different types of lines, which are explained in the legend above the figure.

sources.summary: The paper studies PG 1553+113 as a high-energy-peaked BL Lac object, analyzing 2015 XMM-Newton, Swift, and WEBT data to characterize its SED, variability, polarization, and jet emission.

quotes.quote: The source PG 1553+113 is a high-energy peaked BL Lac object (HBL), which means that its spectral energy distribution (SED) shows a synchrotron bump extending from the radio to the X-rays, and an inverse-Compton bump from X-rays to TeV energies. For these sources, the inverse-Compton radiation is usually assumed to originate from the scattering of synchrotron photons by the same relativistic electrons that produce the synchrotron emission SSC models; e.g. mas97 . The redshift of PG 1553+113 has been investigated in several ways, but has not been firmly established yet.

In 2015, nearly 218 hours of XMM-Newton observing time on PG 1553+113 were awarded to investigate the warm-hot intergalactic medium WHIM; e.g. and references therein nic13 . At the same time, this offered an extraordinary opportunity to study in detail the emission of the blazar itself and, in particular, the problem of the spectral connection between the UV and X-ray regions discussed by rai15 . To maximize the scientific output, the WEBT organized a new multifrequency campaign involving optical, near-infrared, and radio observing facilities.

sources.summary: PG 1553+113 is treated as a distant HBL monitored by MAGIC for over a decade; its quasi-periodic multi-band oscillations are modeled as geometric variability from jet precession.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113 is a distant ( z 0.5 , ) HBL. Quasi-periodic oscillations with a period of 2.18 0.08 year has been observed from this source in GeV, optical and partially also in radio bands . PG 1553+113 is being observed by the MAGIC telescopes already since over 10 years (see Fig. fig:1553 ), however the early observations were irregular and biased by observations of flares.

Regular, denser monitoring is ongoing since 2015. In order to explain the broadband data we consider a geometrical model of emission for periodic blazar . According to it the variability is produced by the modulation of the observation angle due to jet precession.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PG1553+113 as a BL Lac source with a 2.18-year reported Fermi periodicity and matched optical and gamma-ray peaks near 780-810 days.

quotes.quote: provided evidence of a quasi-periodicity of 2.18 yr in the Fermi light curve of PG1553+113 (z 0.4), with counterparts in the optical and radio.

The periodograms of PG1553+113 show peaks at the same frequency within the errors, corresponding to T _ 780 63 \ days and T _ opt1 810 52 days, similarly to the the quasi-periodic signal found by . In this source, higher significances are related to the ray light curve (see Table S ). A second peak in the optical at T _ opt2 250 \ \ 60 days is also apparent. The occurrence of harmonic-related oscillations in the two independent light curves, and optical, in one source, and the concurrence of the same period in the two bands for the other one, although individually modest, suggest that the peaks may be related to a real quasi-periodicities, superposed onto chaotic variabilities. The peaks in the PG1553+113 spectrum at approximately the same frequency in the optical and ray bands appear to be an interesting case, which deserves to be further investigated.

In this paper we have reconsidered the search for year-long quasi-periodicities in optical and rays of the two BL Lac sources PKS2155 304 and PG1553+113, using a procedure adapted from the recipes of and described in our paper on BL Lac Sandrinelli2017 . Our results confirm the indication of year-like quasi-periodicities in PKS2155 304 and PG1553+113, previously obtained following different numerical schemes Schulz2002,Ackermann2015 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Fermi LAT data for PG 1553+113, testing whether relativistic Doppler boosting can explain its gamma-ray quasi-periodic oscillation behavior.

quotes.quote: We analyze the Fermi LAT data of PG 1553+113 spanning from 2008 August until 2018 February. According to 29 four-month average spectral energy distributions (SEDs) in the energy range of 0.1-300 GeV, we split the Fermi LAT energy range into three bands: 0.1-1 GeV, 1-10 GeV, and 10-300 GeV. The spectrum in each energy range can be successfully fitted by a power-law.

The light curves and photon indices in the three energy ranges are obtained. Then, light curves in three narrow energy ranges, i.e., 0.2-0.5 GeV, 2-5 GeV and 20- 40 GeV, are constructed, and the relative variability amplitudes in the three narrow energy ranges are calculated. A discrete-correlation analysis is performed for the light curves.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1ES 1553+113 as a blazar with strong gamma-ray and X-ray emission, a bright soft X-ray continuum, appreciable foreground column density, and distinctive carbon-edge absorption.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1553+113, henceforth referred to as 1ES 1553, is a blazar that emits strongly in both the gamma ray and X-ray regime 18 . 1ES 1553 has a bright soft X-ray continuum and it has a column density appreciably higher than Mrk 421, at N_ H = 3.65 10^ 20 H atoms cm ^ 2 2 , and it is therefore a good candidate for analysis of the carbon K-edge. As should be expected, however, it is not as bright as Mrk 421 and so sensitivity issues will arise as pointed out by 36 .

With our new empirical instrument response model, we are ready to study a source with significant interstellar absorption, 1ES 1553+113. The spectrum of this source, after our reduction procedure described above, if there was no interstellar carbon absorption along the line of sight to 1ES 1553, would be the same as the empirical instrument response we found for Mkn 421. However, we find a noticeable difference, as shown in fig:6 Figure 5 .

sources.summary: The paper presents a coordinated multiwavelength campaign on PG 1553+11, finding X-ray and optical flaring and SED behavior consistent with an extreme X-ray-selected BL Lac object.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+11 was the target of a coordinated three week multiwavelength campaign during 2003 April and May. A significant X-ray flare was observed during the second half of this campaign. Although no optical flare was recorded during the X-ray campaign, optical observations obtained immediately prior to the campaign displayed a higher flux than that recorded during the campaign. An optical flare was observed a few days after the end of the X-ray campaign and may be related to the X-ray flare.

This is the most exciting result from the SED analysis; these values are consistent with PG 1553+11 being an extreme XBL, where the cutoff between RBLs and XBLs is -5.5 and extreme XBLs have a value of -4.5 or greater rec03 . Compared to flux ratios calculated for the previously discussed XBLs (see Table 7), PG 1553+11 is significantly more extreme than Mkn 421 and Mkn 501 even in a low X-ray state, and in most states is more extreme than PKS 2155-304 per96 .

sources.summary: The paper reports that 1553+113 has a faint southern lobe, a northeast VLBA/VLBI jet misaligned with that structure, and polarized core and inner-jet emission.

quotes.quote: 1553+113 The 1.4 GHz VLA image of Rector03 shows a faint lobe south of the core, with a weak ``hot spot'' in PA=160 . The 5 GHz VLBA map however shows a jet extending to the northeast in PA =48 , giving a large misalignment of PA=112 Rector03 . Our VLBI map (Fig.  fig:1553 ) shows a dominant core and a short jet in PA 45 . There may be fainter jet emission further from the core in this same direction, and also possibly a faint extension on the counterjet side. Polarization was detected in both the core and inner jet, but the orientation of the vectors relative to the jet is unclear.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PG 1553+113 as an active galactic nucleus with BL Lac-like spectra, no detected spectral lines, uncertain redshift, and confirmed very-high-energy gamma-ray emission.

quotes.quote: The active galactic nucleus (AGN) PG 1553+113 was first reported in the Palomar-Green catalog of UV-bright objects . Its spectral characteristics are close to those of X-ray selected BL Lacs . Despite several attempts, no emission or absorption lines have been found in the spectrum of PG 1553+113. Thus only indirect methods can be used to determine the redshift z . Recently the H.E.S.S. and MAGIC collaborations have presented ray signals at the >4 and the 8.8 level, respectively, making this source a confirmed VHE ray emitter.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first detailed GeV study of PG 1553+113, finding steady hard-spectrum emission, modeling its broadband SED, and interpreting its large gamma-ray spectral break as EBL absorption.

quotes.quote: We report the observations of PG 1553+113 during the first 200 days of science operations, from 4 August 2008 to 22 February 2009 (MJD 54682.7-54884.2). This is the first detailed study of PG 1553+113 in the GeV gamma-ray regime and it allows us to fill a gap of three decades in energy in its spectral energy distribution. We find PG 1553+113 to be a steady source with a hard spectrum that is best fit by a simple power-law in the energy band. We combine the data with archival radio, optical, X-ray and very high energy (VHE) gamma-ray data to model its broadband spectral energy distribution and find that a simple, one-zone synchrotron self-Compton model provides a reasonable fit. PG 1553+113 has the softest VHE spectrum of all sources detected in that regime and, out of those with significant detections across the energy bandpass so far, the hardest spectrum in that energy regime.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1553+113 as a ray-bright BL Lac and persistent VHE source; its RINGO2/KVA data agree with sparse literature and show a 2012 EVPA rotation during a flare.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113 PG 1553+113 is a ray bright BL Lac object at z 0.395 danforth2010 . It is a persistent source of VHE rays albert2007a,aharonian2006 which has triggered several multi-wavelength studies of the source (e.g. ). However, only few campaigns have included polarimetric observations. Polarisation observations were reported in , and with the maximum value for polarisation degree of 8.2 .

RINGO2 and KVA-60 data suffer from poor sampling but agree with literature, showing an EVPA which is 100-150 ^ until March 2012 when there is a rotation over a period of a few months which coincides with a flare detected in HESS and MAGIC but not in Fermi abramowski2015,aleksic2015 .

sources.summary: The paper confirms PG 1553+113 as a Fermi-LAT blazar with a previously reported quasi-periodic gamma-ray modulation and high statistical periodicity evidence.

quotes.quote: The second class consists of blazars, showing strong evidence for quasi-periodic modulations in , including PG 1553+113 2.2 year period, pg1553 , PKS 2155-304 1.7 years, pks2155p1, pks2155p2 , and BL Lacertae 1.9 years, bllac . Modulated signals from these sources were detected by Fermi LAT.

Three of these blazars are PG 1553+113, BL Lacertae, and PKS 2155-304, whose periodicities were previously reported in the literature. The computed periods (see Table Tab ) agree with those previously reported. These three blazars show the highest statistical chances to be periodic sources.

sources.summary: The paper uses 1ES 1553+113 as a z~0.4 blazar sightline, associating reported WHIM absorption features and WISE-SuperCOSMOS density peaks at z=0.23 and z=0.31 with intervening filaments.

quotes.quote: We report the a posteriori confirmation of two large-scale filaments along the sight-line of the blazar 1ES 1553+113, which correspond to Warm-Hot Intergalactic Medium WHIM; cen99,dave01 absorption features in the X-ray and far ultraviolet. reported 5 low signal-to-noise absorption features along the sight line to the z 0.4 blazar 1ES 1553+113.

Figure 1 shows the WISE-SuperCOSMOS maps in the 25 ^ x 25 ^ field surrounding 1ES 1553+113, plotted over the eight bins that cover the complete redshift range of the survey. We note that the 2.5 ^ Gaussian smoothing corresponds to 11-16 Mpc at the highest redshifts, and highlights only the largest-scale filaments and super-clusters e.g., ryu03 . Due to the varying completeness we've normalized the maps in each bin. Using the pixel value of these normalized maps at the location of 1ES 1553+113, the largest intervening structure is at 0.21<z<0.25 , followed by 0.29<z<0.33 . To explore these structures in more detail, we have re-binned the catalog into z = 0.02 slices, and plotted the pixel value along the l.o.s. to 1ES 1553+113 as a function of redshift (Fig. 1 bottom, solid blue line), along with the mean (dashed green line) and rms (shaded blue region) of the pixel values in the 25 ^ x 25 ^ images after convolution.

sources.summary: The paper observes BL Lac object PG 1553+113 after reported activity, measuring multi-band polarization above earlier values and interpreting it as intrinsic variability.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113 PG 1553+113 (also known as HESS J1555+111) is a well known BL Lac object first discovered in the Palomar-Green survey Green1986 . The source periodically enters outburst and produces very high energy emission (see Aharonian2006,Abdo2010,Aleksic2015 ). We observed the source in response to a report of recent activity on 2016 April 27 Kapanadze2016 . We observed the source approximately seven weeks post-alert and measured a polarisation of P = 5.15( 0.09) , P = 5.26( 0.13) and P = 4.78( 0.07) with angles of = 31.50( 0.49) deg, = 31.55( 0.73) deg and = 30.62( 0.43) deg in the V , B and R bands respectively. Our measurements are significantly higher than the 2 polarisation reported by Andruchow2011 in the B and R bands.

sources.summary: The paper confirms a 2.2 yr gamma-ray periodicity for PG 1553+113, notes prior detections and one contrary study, and uses it to test how upper limits affect period recovery.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113: ackermann_pg1553 , tavani_pdm_pg_1553 , prokhorov_set and sandrinelli_powerlaw find a period of 2.2 yr with high significance, compatible with the result found in the present work of 2.2 yr. covino_negation finds no periodic ray emission for this object.

In this section, we use PG 1553+113 to evaluate the impact in the period detection of the existence of upper limits in the LCs. PG 1553+113 is used because it is detected in each time bin and also it features the most significant periodicity.
To calculate the loss of significance, we consider the period of 2.2 yr and the significance level of > 4 as reference values.

sources.summary: The paper finds PG 1553+113 has the strongest periodic signal in the AGN sample, with a 774-day period, a clear linear trend, and a small autoregressive noise term.

quotes.quote: The most significant periodic signal is that for PG 1553+113, with a p_ value of 1.2 10^ 7 denoting a very strong evidence of the source's periodic behaviour. The period found of 774 days( 2.1 years) is compatible with previous periodicity studies on the source. Besides, the light-curve shows a clear linear trend C= 9 10^ 5 and a small noise/auto-regressive term _1 = 0.22 . The inclusion of a linear trend in the fit of the source's behaviour is also fundamental in terms of significance of periodicity. In the fit without a linear trend, the same period is found with a much lower p_ value '= 3.4 10^ 4 and a much higher noise term _1' = 0.49 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses PG1553+113 as a BL Lac/blazar case where Doppler boosting, gamma-ray/optical modulation, periodic multi-wavelength flaring, and possible supermassive binary signatures point to jet precession.

quotes.quote: For PG 1553+113, the Doppler boosting factor is superposed to the Fermi ray light curve see Fig. 2, bottom panels in Caproni2017 .

stamerra use multi-wavelength observations of PG1553+113 to investigate if the observed modulation (gamma-rays, optical) can be explained with geometrical variations in the jet, possibly pointing to jet precession. They discuss PG1553+113 as a probe for a geometrical periodic modulation. Independent evidence for jet precession being responsible for the periodic multi-wavelength flaring in PG1553+113 has been presented by Caproni2017 . tavani also discuss the possible gamma-ray (from Fermi LAT observations) signatures of a SMBBH in PG 1553+113.

sources.summary: The paper presents PG 1553+113 as a high-synchrotron-peak blazar with measured X-ray polarization, lower radio/optical polarization, and an orphan optical polarization swing.

quotes.quote: Here, we present the first X-ray polarization observations of PG 1553+113, a high-synchrotron-peak blazar observed by the Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer ( ). We detect an X-ray polarization degree of (10 2) along an electric-vector position angle of _X=86^ 8^ . At the same time, the radio and optical polarization degrees are lower by a factor of 3. During our \ pointing, we observed the first orphan optical polarization swing of the era, as the optical angle of PG 1553+113 underwent a smooth monotonic rotation by about 125 ^ , with a rate of 17 degree per day. We do not find evidence of a similar rotation in either radio or X-rays, which suggests that the X-ray and optically emitting regions are separate or, at most, partially co-spatial.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 1553+113 as a bright XBL/HBL with unknown redshift and unresolved host, observed by several X-ray instruments and detected by Suzaku above 30 keV.

quotes.quote: Owing to the non-detection of any lines nor resolving its host galaxy the redshift of the XBL/HBL 1ES 1553+113 is to date still unknown.

Like 1ES 1101-232, 1ES 1553+113 is a strong X-ray source and has been visited by several X-ray instruments, however, focusing mainly towards the soft part of its X-ray spectrum (Einstein, ROSAT, BeppoSAX, RXTE, XMM).
Results We detected 1ES 1101-232 and 1ES 1553+113 with Suzaku up to energies above 30 keV. This is the highest X-ray energy measurement so far obtained for 1ES 1553+113.

sources.summary: The paper models PG 1553+113 as an HBL with HESS, MAGIC, Fermi/LAT, KVA, and Suzaku observations, unknown redshift, and no strong or fast X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113. Its VHE emission with a very soft spectrum (photon index of =4.0 0.6 ) was detected in 2005 by HESS, and no evidence of variability was found (Aharonian et al. 2006a). The VHE emission was subsequently confirmed by MAGIC (Albert et al. 2007c), and the integral flux levels were consistent with that of HESS. PG 1553+113 is in the Fermi LAT bright AGN source list, but was not detected by EGRET because it was in a low state during the observation. This source is a HBL object and a bright X-ray source with many observations, but no strong or fast variability was detected in the X-ray band (Reimer et al. 2008).

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1553+113 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113 : The source is one of the brightest ones in optical band while in radio its average flux density is around the median of the sample. The optical light curve shows several clear outbursts and the modulation index is large, while in radio the appearance of the light curve is smoother and modulation index small. In the beginning of both light curves there seems to be a decreasing trend ending around MJD 55200 (see Fig. Fig:lc23 ). Analysis of revealed a two-year periodicity in the ray light curve and visually this periodicity is apparent also in our light curves.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES1553+113 as an X-ray-bright blazar sightline, revisits prior absorption features possibly associated with WHIM, and analyzes 23 RGS observations totaling 1.8 Ms.

quotes.quote: conducted a blind search for X-ray absorption lines in the \ spectrum of the X--ray bright blazar , and identified two absorption features interpreted as \ He-- absorbers at z 0.4339 and 0.3557 possibly associated with the WHIM towards the blazar.

We analyzed all RGS observations of 1ES1553+113 available as of May 2022, for a total of 23 observations A short observation (ObsID: 081083701) with exposure of 8 ks is discarded due to incomplete observation files. with total RGS exposure time of 1.8 Ms. The observations are reported in Table tab:Data .

sources.summary: The paper gives PG 1553+113 an individual blazar variability note, summarizing TeV-band detections, GeV or hard-X-ray behavior, and variability classification.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113 : This bright VHE emitting blazar has exhibited quasi-periodic variability in the ray band 2015ApJ...813L..41A . It was detected almost throughout all the analyzed periods and has been detected with several Cherenkov telescopes Figure fig:1 ; 2012ApJ...748...46A,2015ApJ...799....7A,2015ApJ...802...65A,2017A&A...600A..89H,2019ICRC...36..689G . Based on the value, this object is partially variable in the TeV band. Several episodes of moderate amplitude, i.e., a factor of 2 with respect to the 15 years averaged flux, flaring activity have been noticed which were also reflected in the GeV band light curve.

sources.summary: The paper reports a statistically insignificant negative global regression for PG 1553+113, while shorter intervals showed distinct states and about one magnitude flux variation.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113: The source was observed during 294 nights in 2007--2012. The global regression fit indicates negative though statistically not significant CM correlation, in agreement with the results by ; however, in shorter time intervals distinct states following or trends can be seen. About 1 mag flux variations corresponded to the relatively narrow, _ BR 1.5-2.0 range covered.

sources.summary: The paper reports PG 1553+113 as the only source with a marginal p-value, assumes a 10 TeV cutoff for cascade modeling, and derives upper limits.

quotes.quote: Only PG 1553+113 has a marginal p-value, which does not account for the zenith correction or systematic uncertainties.
For VER J0521+211, 1ES 0229+200, and PG 1553+113, E_ cut is taken to be 10 TeV and the fraction of the total emission due to cascade emission is within VERITAS sensitivity (cascade fraction > 5 ).
Upper limits were derived for PG 1553+113 and VER J0521+211 as well, however 1ES 0229+200 allows the strongest constraints on f_ compared to the prediction.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PG 1553+113 as an HSP TeV BL Lac source with EVPA rotations detected in 2013 and 2014 and included among significant rotating sources.

quotes.quote: During the first observing season of RoboPol in 2013, we detected a 128 degree EVPA rotation in the HSP source PG 1553+113 blinov15 .
The same source was seen to rotate during 2014 as well by about 145 degrees, as reported in , confirming the TeV HSPs as a class of objects with EVPA rotations (see also jermak16 ).

We find significant rotations in three of the HSP TeV sources (RGB J0136+391, PG 1553+113, and 1ES 1727+502), and nine rotations in six non-TeV sources (GB6 J1037+5711,GB6 J1542+6129, TXS 1557+565, 87GB 164812.2+524023, RXJ 1809.3+2041, S5 2023+760).

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1553+113 as a BL Lac target with uncertain redshift, extracting extended-emission flux limits and noting a data-simulation width tension resolved by systematics.

quotes.quote: For PG 1553+113, the source redshift is not well constrained, making it difficult to estimate the impact of the EBL absorption on the intrinsic VHE spectrum and consequently to check for the presence of a spectral break. Consequently, only limits on the flux due to extended emission are extracted for PG 1553+113, which does not require an assumption about the energy cutoff.

Only for PG 1553+113 does the p value indicate a mismatch between data and simulation, at the 3 level, a tension that is resolved when the systematic uncertainties and zenith correction are considered.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1ES 1553+113 as the bright blazar at the XMM175UNDF center, gives its coordinates and X-ray flux, and masks it because its artifacts affect source detection.

quotes.quote: The present XMM-Newton survey comprises 13 observations taken in 2015 and 2017 covering an area of 30 30 centered at the blazar 1ES 1553+113 (RA = 238^ 55 45.48 , DEC = 11^ 11 24.36 ) Nicastro2018 .

Since every observation is centered around the blazar 1ES 1553+113 ( F_ 0.2-12 keV 2 10 ^ 11 erg s^ 1 cm^ 2 ), we decided to set a circular mask of 3 radius centered on 1ES 1553+113 to avoid false identifications of sources, due to the star-like pattern created by the spider structure which supports the mirrors of the telescope (see Figure  fig3 ).

Both catalogs contain the same 13 pointings used in our analysis, with the addition of 10 extra PN small-window calibration observations (each of 30ks ) pointed on the blazar 1ES 1553+113 that our survey does not include.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PG 1553+113 gamma-ray observations for ALP-photon oscillations, finding some parameter regions slightly improve the spectral fit without setting a limit.

quotes.quote: We explore the axion-like particle (ALP)-photon oscillation effect in the ray spectra of the blazars Markarian 421 (Mrk 421) and PG 1553+113, which are measured by the Major Atmospheric Gamma Imaging Cherenkov Telescopes (MAGIC) and Fermi Large Area Telescope ( Fermi LAT) with high precision. We investigate the constraints on the ALP parameter space using the Mrk 421 and PG 1553+113 observations of 15 and 5 phases, respectively.
We also find that the ALP hypothesis can slightly improve the fit to the PG 1553+113 results in some parameter regions, and do not set the limit in this case.

sources.summary: PG 1553+113 is treated as an HSP blazar with moderate X-ray variability, long-gap monitoring uncertainties, and one of the highest predicted average neutrino rates in the sample.

quotes.quote: Such month-long blocks are a result of large gaps between observations (see e.g. the light curve of PG 1553+113 in Fig.  fig:lc ) caused by the lack of all-sky X-ray monitoring.

Continuing with this idea another interesting HSP blazar for X-ray monitoring is PG 1553+113 with moderate X-ray variability (duty cycle 13 per cent) and a total number of 0.6 predicted muon and antimuon neutrinos. In addition, is one of the blazars with the highest average rate of muon and antimuon neutrinos from flares in our sample with N _ _ + _ 0.25  yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper reviews previous QPO claims for PG 1553+113 near 800 days, but finds its own candidate drops below significance after trial correction.

quotes.quote: PG 1553+113: The first detection of a periodic behaviour in the emission of this high-synchrotron-peak BL Lac object was claimed by , with a period of 798 d. Some more recent studies found a periodic feature at similar values: reported two peaks at same frequency 780 810 d, detected a period of 790 d, claimed a possible evidence at period 800 d, and found high significance level at period 803 d. Our results show that this QPO candidate is significant in pre-trial significance (at a confidence level of 3 ( 2 ) for the monthly (weekly) binned light curve), but becomes less than 1 after trial correction consistent with the results by AitBenkhali20 .

gmw_00000594.name: J0840+5624
gmw_00000594.overview: J0840+5624 is a quasar host galaxy at a redshift of 5.8441 [arXiv:1002.1561]. It features a broad, double-peaked CO line profile [arXiv:1002.1561], marginal dust continuum emission [arXiv:1002.1561], and no evidence of excess low-excitation CO (2-1) line emission [arXiv:1105.4199].

sources.summary: The paper detects CO in the z~6 quasar host J0840+5624, finding a broad double-peaked line, a host-galaxy redshift of 5.8441, and tentative dust continuum.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present new observations of CO (6-5) and/or (5-4) line emission in another six z 6 quasars (J0840+5624, J1044 0125, J1048+4637, J1335+3533, J1425+3254, J2054 0005), using the Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI). They all have 250 GHz flux density of S_ 250GHz 1.8 mJy and corresponding FIR luminosities of L_ FIR >5 10^ 12 L_ (using the estimation given in Wang et al. 2008a and 2008b).

Both the CO (6-5) and (5-4) transitions were detected, peaking at 08^ h 40^ m 35.08^ s , +56 24'20.5'' , 0.6'' from the optical quasar position. The integrated line flux densities are 0.60 0.07 Jy km s ^ 1 and 0.72 0.15 Jy km s ^ 1 , respectively. A single Gaussian line profile fitted to the combined spectrum of the two CO transitions yields a host galaxy redshift of 5.8441 0.0013 and a FWHM line width of 860 190 km s ^ 1 . This is the largest CO line width that we have detected among the z 6 quasars, and the line profile is double-peaked.

The 350 m dust continuum emission is marginally detected in this object at 2.9 . The continuum source is un-resolved and peaks at RA= 08^ h 40^ m 35.32^ s Dec=56d24 ' 17.5 '' , which is 3.1 '' away from the optical quasar (the left panel of figure 2).

sources.summary: The paper models QSO J0840+5624 with a massive high-redshift galaxy scenario, matching observed quantities at 170 Myr or, with a larger galaxy, at 400 Myr.

quotes.quote: At an epoch of 170 Myr the system with M_ ini = 3 10^ 11 is plausible for the QSO J0840+5624 fan06 at z = 5.85, if an inclination angle higher than the assumed 40 is assumed. The SFR is 1500 yr ^ 1 and the metallicity is 4 . The stellar mass is around 1.1 10^ 11 . The amount of dust obtained with a `high' SN efficiency is 2.1 10^ 8 , while with the `maximum' SN efficiency the dust mass exceeds a few times 10^ 9 (as already at an epoch of 100 Myr). However, for a L'_ CO(1- 0) = 3.2 10 ^ 10 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 as derived for this QSO the gas-to-H _2 ratio of _ g,H_ 2 5--7 for = 0.8--1 (K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 ) ^ 1 is higher than for the less massive galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper reports that J0840+5624 has prior dust and high-J CO detections, but its EVLA CO(2-1) signal is only marginal and shows no low-excitation excess.

quotes.quote: J0840+5624 This source was detected in (sub)mm dust continuum emission and CO (6-5) and (5-4) line emission; it has the broadest line width, FWHM=860 km s^ 1 , among the CO-detected z 6 quasars wang07,wang10 . We observed the line at the redshift of z=5.8441 0.0013 derived from the high-order CO detections and find no clear detection in a velocity-averaged map averaging over 1070 km s^ 1 made at the full resolution of 1.09'' 0.76'' . At a lower resolution of 2.19'' 1.96'' , marginal signal ( 2.8 ) appears on the map (Figure 1), with a double-peaked mophology along the east-west direction.
Our observations show no evidence of excess CO (2-1) line emission and additional low excitation component in the host galaxy of J0840+5624.

gmw_00000595.name: J1044 0125
gmw_00000595.overview: J1044 0125 is a highly luminous broad-absorption-line quasar at a redshift of approximately 5.8 [arXiv:astro-ph/0005414, arXiv:astro-ph/0104147]. The host galaxy exhibits turbulent gas kinematics with no clear signs of rotation [arXiv:1302.4154, arXiv:1904.07749], and it may host an infalling companion or outflow node located 4.9 kiloparsecs to the east [arXiv:1904.07749].

sources.summary: The paper detects CO(6-5) from J1044 0125, measuring the narrowest CO line in the z~6 sample and placing limits on continuum and 350 micron emission.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: peaking at the position of 10^ h 44^ m 33.02 , 01 25'02.3''

quotes.quote: In this work, we present new observations of CO (6-5) and/or (5-4) line emission in another six z 6 quasars (J0840+5624, J1044 0125, J1048+4637, J1335+3533, J1425+3254, J2054 0005), using the Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI). They all have 250 GHz flux density of S_ 250GHz 1.8 mJy and corresponding FIR luminosities of L_ FIR >5 10^ 12 L_ (using the estimation given in Wang et al. 2008a and 2008b).

We detected the CO (6-5) line in this source, peaking at the position of 10^ h 44^ m 33.02 , 01 25'02.3'' , 0.3'' away from the optical quasar. The FWHM of the CO line fitted with a single Gaussian profile is 160 60 km s ^ 1 , which represents the narrowest CO line width among all the CO-detected z 6 quasars. The integrated line flux is 0.21 0.04 Jy km s^ 1 . The 3 upper limit on the continuum emission is 0.15 mJy at 102 GHz.
The 350 m dust continuum emission has not been detected in our SHARC-II observations, and the 1 rms is 4.5 mJy beam ^ 1 . This yields a 3 flux density upper limit of 13.5 mJy.

sources.summary: The paper includes SDSSp J104433.04--012502.2 as a z=5.8 quasar in the complete sample, noting its ionized-IGM constraint and massive black hole implications.

quotes.quote: The lack of a Gunn-Peterson trough (Shklovsky 1964, Scheuer 1965, Gunn Peterson 1965) in the spectrum of the luminous quasar SDSSp J104433.04--012502.2 The naming convention for the SDSS sources is SDSSp JHHMMSS.SS DDMMSS.S, where ``p'' stands for the preliminary SDSS astrometry, and the positions are expressed in J2000.0 coordinates. The astrometry is accurate to better than 0.2'' in each coordinate. at z=5.8 (SDSS 1044--0125 for brevity, , for an updated redshift of this object, see and ) indicates that the universe was already highly ionized at that redshift.

Assuming that SDSS 1044-0125 is radiating at the Eddington luminosity, this object contains a central black hole of several billion solar masses. The assembly of such massive objects in a timescale shorter than 1 Gyr yields constraints on models of the formation of massive black holes (e.g., ). The abundance and evolution of such quasars can provide sensitive tests for models of quasar and galaxy evolution.
The three new objects, together with the previously published z=5.8 quasar SDSSp J104433.04-012502.2, form a complete color-selected flux-limited sample at z 5.8 .

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS1044-01 as the most distant HiBAL quasar, refitting its spectrum at z=5.78 and finding little dust reddening, a BAL-like X-ray index, and a CIV balnicity index.

quotes.quote: SDSS1044-01: the most distant HiBAL quasar (z=5.78) The Amici+NICS spectrum of this quasar was already shown in Maiolino et al. ( ). We present again the spectrum here to extract the continuum slope and balnicity index with the same template spectrum as the other quasars. In Fig.  fig3 we show the observed spectrum along with the SDSS non-BAL template (thin line).

Our spectrum is best fit by a redshift of 5.78 rather than 5.74 derived previously, but the difference my be caused by the blending with the absorption features. The continuum slope appears only slightly redder than the average of the SDSS non-BAL quasars: = -1.55 (though consistent with the unreddened value of -1.62 within the uncertainties). Note that in Maiolino et al. ( ) we found a better match (both for the continuum and for the emission lines) with the QSO template obtained by Francis et al. ( ).

The balnicity index Goodrich et al. ( ) obtain a much lower balnicity index for this quasar because they integrated starting from 5000 km/s blueward of the peak, while the formal balnicity index has to be calculated starting 3000 km/s blueward of the peak (Weymann et al. ). is 1950 250 km/s.

sources.summary: The paper resolves SDSS J1044-0125 as a luminous z~6 quasar host with turbulent [C II] kinematics, no clear rotation, and a possible eastern companion or outflow node.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The quasar coordinates for SDSS 1044-0125 are RA=10 ^ h 44 ^ m 33.041 ^ s , and DEC=-01 ^d 25 ' 02.09 '' , with an uncertainty of 27 mas in RA and 20 mas in DEC.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1044-0125 and ULAS J1319+0950 are among the most optically luminous quasars at z 6 with rest-frame 1450 apparent magnitudes of m_ 1450 =19.21 and 19.65, respectively fan00,mortlock09 . The SMBH mass is (2.7 0.6) 10^ 9 M_ for ULAS J1319+0950 shao17 and (5.6 0.6) 10^ 9 M_ for SDSS J1044-0125 shen19 .

The [ C II ] emitting gas in the central region of SDSS J1044-0125 does not show a clear sign of rotation. The PV-diagram of the [ C II ] emission suggests very turbulent gas kinematics in the central area. The [ C II ] line spectrum shows multiple peaks, which is broader and slightly offset from the CO (6-5) line emission. In addition, we tentatively detect an offset peak, 4.9 kpc away to the east of the quasar with a very broad line width ( 750 190 km s^ 1 ) which may relate to an infalling companion source or node of outflows.

sources.summary: The appendix says this quasar needed unweighted continuum fitting, iron-template assumptions, restricted fitting of a partly absorbed line, and caution because the complex is broad.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1044--0125 To properly fit the continuum over the entire wavelength range, it was necessary to disable the fit weights for the continuum model. In this spectrum the line falls into one of the telluric absorption bands. To properly fit the continuum, including the iron contribution, we have assumed a FWHM for the iron template of FWHM _ FeII =2500 km s ^ 1 and set the redshift to the systemic redshift of the quasar.

The line is partially absorbed by a strong blue-ward absorption trough. Thus, we restricted the line fit to the red-ward half of the line and approximated the line using only one Gaussian component. The complex has a very broad structure in this spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSSp J104433.04--012502.2 as a luminous z=5.80 i-band-dropout quasar whose spectrum shows Ly emission, strong absorption, metal emission, and no Gunn-Peterson trough.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: SDSSp J104433.04--012502.2 (the name reflecting its J2000 coordinates from the preliminary SDSS astrometry, accurate to 0.1'' in each coordinate)

quotes.quote: We present observations of SDSSp J104433.04--012502.2, a luminous quasar at z=5.80 discovered from Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) multicolor imaging data. This object was selected as an i' band dropout object, with i^*=21.8 0.2 , z^*=19.2 0.1 . It has an absolute magnitude M_ 1450 = -27.2 ( H_ 0 =50 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , q_ 0 = 0.5 ).

The spectrum shows a strong and broad Ly emission line, strong Ly forest absorption lines with a mean continuum decrement D_ A = 0.91 , and a Lyman Limit System at z=5.72 . The spectrum also shows strong OI and SiIV emission lines similar to those of quasars at z 5 , suggesting that these metals were produced at redshift beyond six. The lack of a Gunn-Peterson trough in the spectrum indicates that the universe is already highly ionized at z 5.8 .

sources.summary: The paper presents J1044 0125 as a z~6 broad-absorption-line quasar with a massive black hole, detected [C II] emission, unresolved continuum, and no velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: J1044 0125 . This is another broad absorption line quasar at z 6 discovered from the SDSS main survey with m_ 1450 =19.2 fan00 . A deep near-IR spectrum of the quasar C IV line emission of this object yields a SMBH mass of 10.5 10^ 9 M_ (Jiang et al. 2007). The beam size of the [ C II ] line observation is 0.66'' 0.45'' and the deconvolved source size is estimated to be (0.61'' 0.11'') (0.33'' 0.22'') oriented at PA=65^ 48^ . No gradient is seen in the line intensity-weighted velocity map of this object. The 287 GHz continuum flux density is 3.12 0.09 mJy and the source is unresolved in the continuum.

sources.summary: SDSS J104433.04 012502.2 is described as a z=5.80 X-ray-weak quasar with strong CIV absorption, likely a BAL QSO obscured by Compton-thick gas but showing little dust reddening.

quotes.quote: We report the near-IR detection of a prominent CIV absorption in the rest-frame UV spectrum of the most distant known QSO, SDSS J104433.04 012502.2, at z=5.80 . This QSO was recently observed with XMM-Newton and it was found to be notably X-ray weak. The equivalent width of the CIV absorption feature ( 10 ) strongly supports the idea that the X-ray faintness of this QSO is due to heavy absorption by gas with a column density N_H > 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 . The shape of the CIV feature suggests that this is a Broad Absorption Line QSO. Although absorbed by a huge column of gas, the observed continuum in the 0.9--2.4 m range ( 1300--3500 \ rest frame) exactly matches the template of unabsorbed QSOs without invoking any reddening ( E_ B-V <0.08 mag), indicating that dust in the absorbing gas is either absent or composed of large grains.

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSSp J104433.04 012502.2 as the z=5.74 quasar field, discussing its proximity to LAE J1044 0123 and estimating its Stromgren radius.

quotes.quote: Using the prime-focus camera, Suprime-Cam, on the 8.2 m Subaru telescope together with a narrow-passband filter centered at _ = 8150 \ with passband of = 120 , we have obtained a very deep image of the field surrounding the quasar SDSSp J104433.04 012502.2 at a redshift of 5.74.

Since the redshift of LAE J1044 0123 ( z=5.655 ) is close to that of SDSSp J104433.04 012502.2 ( z=5.74 ), it is possible that these two objects are located at nearly the same cosmological distance. The angular separation between LAE J1044 0123 and SDSSp J104433.04 012502.2, 113 arcsec, corresponds to the linear separation of 4.45 h_ 0.7 ^ 1 Mpc. The Str omgren radius of SDSSp J104433.04 012502.2 can be estimated to be r_ S 6.3 (t_ Q /2 10^7 yr )^ 1/3 Mpc using equation (1) in Haiman Cen (2002) where t_ Q is the lifetime of the quasar (see also Cen Haiman 2000).

gmw_00000596.name: J1048+4637
gmw_00000596.overview: J1048+4637 is a broad-absorption line quasar located at a redshift of approximately 6.2 [arXiv:1002.1561, arXiv:astro-ph/0312402]. Its host galaxy contains a dust mass exceeding 10^8 solar masses [arXiv:1410.7861], with carbonaceous grains primarily in the form of amorphous carbon [arXiv:1410.7861].

sources.summary: The paper detects CO and millimeter continuum in J1048+4637, measuring redshift 6.2284, line width and flux, plus a likely CO(3-2) detection.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present new observations of CO (6-5) and/or (5-4) line emission in another six z 6 quasars (J0840+5624, J1044 0125, J1048+4637, J1335+3533, J1425+3254, J2054 0005), using the Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI). They all have 250 GHz flux density of S_ 250GHz 1.8 mJy and corresponding FIR luminosities of L_ FIR >5 10^ 12 L_ (using the estimation given in Wang et al. 2008a and 2008b).

We detected the CO (6-5) line emission in this source, peaking at 10^ h 48^ m 45.08 , 46 37'18.7'' , 0.5'' away from the optical quasar position. The CO redshift is 6.2284 0.0017, which is close to the value of 6.23 estimated from the Ly + NV emission in the quasar rest-frame UV spectrum (Fan et al. 2003), but higher than the value of 6.19 6.20 measured from the Mg II 2798 emission (Iwamuro et al. 2004; Maiolino et al. 2004). The 3 mm dust continuum emission is detected along with the CO line emission. A simultaneous fit of the CO lines and the continuum gives a continuum flux density of 0.13 0.03 mJy at 96 GHz. The integrated line flux is 0.27 0.05 Jy km s ^ 1 , and the FWHM line width from single Gaussian fit is 370 130 km s ^ 1 .
Thus the 0.30 mJy detection in this channel are likely to be from the CO (3-2) line emission.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS1048+46 as the highest-redshift BAL known, a z=6.22 LoBAL with deep CIV absorption, low-ionization absorption, a blue continuum, high balnicity, and possible redshifted absorption.

quotes.quote: SDSS1048+46: the most distant LoBAL quasar (z=6.22) At a redshift of 6.22 this quasar is now the highest redshift BAL known. Fan et al. ( ) suggested that, based on a hint of SiIV absorption in the optical spectrum, this might be a BAL. The Amici+NICS spectrum is shown in Fig.  fig1 a and clearly displays a very deep and very broad CIV absorption.

The absorption profile is resolved and displays a clear double trough, a feature which is commonly observed in LoBALs. The two main troughs have a blueshift of 11000 and 20000 km/s, which are labeled as systems 1 and 2 , respectively, in Fig.  fig1 a. At the lower velocity shift (11000 km/s) there is also a deep SiIV absorption and absorption of AlIII ] and MgII, which identify this object as a LoBAL.
The most intriguing case is the most distant LoBAL (SDSS1048+46 at z=6.22, Fig.  fig1 ) which is much bluer than any LoBAL found at z < 4.

sources.summary: The paper considers J1048+4637 at z=6.23 and says a lower-initial-SFR model can match its star formation, metallicity, dust mass, and gas-to-H2 ratio constraints.

quotes.quote: In view of this we find that this scenario at an epoch of 100 Myr is more appropriate for the QSOs J1048+4637 fan03 at z = 6.23 and J2054-0005 jiang08 at z = 6.06.

For J1048+4637 the model for a lower initial SFR of _ ini = 10^ 3 yr ^ 1 might be an option. The SFR is 610 yr ^ 1 and the metallicity is 3.4 . While the stellar mass remains low, M_ 2.8 10^ 10 , a dust mass of M_ d 3.5 10^ 8 is obtained for a `maximum' SN efficiency and moderate dust destruction in the ISM. However, for = 0.8--1 (K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 ) ^ 1 the gas-to-H _2 ratio is 5--6, since for the lower initial SFR the system at this epoch is less exhausted.

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J1048+4637 as the high-redshift quasar extinction-curve benchmark, fitting its unusual UV curve with amorphous carbon grains and a high molecular-cloud fraction.

quotes.quote: Maiolino et al. \ (2004) found that the quasar SDSS J1048+4637 (hereafter J1048+4637) at z=6.2 exhibits an unusual UV extinction curve, being flat at wavelengths 1700 and rising at 1700 .

The UV extinction curve in J1048+4637 is successfully reproduced by the models of dust produced by Type II supernovae (SNe II) (Maiolino et al. \ 2004; Hirashita et al. \ 2005; Bianchi Schneider 2007; Hirashita et al. \ 2008), which seems to support the idea that the interstellar dust in such early epochs was predominantly supplied by SNe II generating from massive stars with short lifetimes. On the other hand, a significant fraction of high- z quasars, including J1048+4637, manifest the presence of dust mass in excess of 10^8 M_ in their host galaxies (e.g. \ Calura et al. \ 2014).

As is evident from Figure 4, changing the properties of carbonaceous materials with a large _ MC ( 0.5 ) leads to a successful fit to the high- z quasar extinction curve represented by the one in SDSS J1048+4637. The above results imply that the primary form of carbonaceous grains in high- z dusty quasars is amorphous carbon, and that the fraction of molecular clouds is higher than 0.5 in the host galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J1048+4637 as a z=6.23 quasar with follow-up DIS and HET spectra, a weak Ly+NV line, and a redshift possibly biased by NV absorption.

quotes.quote: The discovery spectrum of SDSS J1048+4637 was obtained with DIS on the ARC 3.5m on 19 May 2002 with a total exposure time of 3600 sec under partly cloudy skies. Figure 5(b) shows a HET/LRS spectrum taken on 1 June 2002 with a total exposure time of 3600 second. This object also has a relatively weak Ly +NV emission line, with a rest-frame equivalent width of 40 , compared to an average of 70 \ at z 4 . Using the peak of the Ly +NV emission and the onset of the Lyman break, we estimate the redshift to be z=6.23 0.05 . Similar to the case of SDSS J1148+5251, this estimate is uncertain and could be biased due to the presence of strong NV absorption.

sources.summary: The paper images the z=6.20 quasar field, identifies nearby foreground galaxies, and concludes the closest possible lens gives only a small magnification with negligible effect on luminosity estimates.

quotes.quote: We present observations for three quasars: SDSS J103027.10+052455.0 (SDSS J1030+0524; z=6.30 ), SDSS J104845.05+463718.3 (SDSS J1048+4637; z=6.20 ) and SDSS J114816.64+525150.3 (SDSS J1148+5251; z=6.42 ). Three galaxies were detected in the fields of each of SDSS J1030+0524 (labelled a, b, ) and SDSS J1048+4637 (d, e, f) and none in the field of SDSS J1148+5251. For SDSS J1048+4637, the closest galaxy (d) is only 1.7 from the quasar.

It is clear that, whatever the redshift of this lensing galaxy, it is not very massive and provides only a small magnification to the flux of SDSS J1048+4637. Therefore it makes a negligible difference to the derived luminosity and black hole mass.

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J104845.05+463718.3 as a z=6.2 BAL quasar whose extinction curve differs from lower-redshift BAL quasars and favors unmixed SN II dust.

quotes.quote: The highest-redshift BAL quasar in their sample, SDSS J104845.05+463718.3 (hereafter ) at z=6.2 shows a red spectrum at the restframe wavelength <1700 . (In this paper, all the wavelengths are shown in the restframe of observed galaxies.) However, at >1700 , there is no indication of reddening. Then they suggest the extinction curve to be flat at 1700 \ and rising at 1700 .
Maiolino et al. \ (2004b) find that the extinction curve of \ is different from that of low- z BAL quasars and is in excellent agreement with the SN II dust models by Todini Ferrara (2001).

gmw_00000597.name: J1335+3533
gmw_00000597.overview: J1335+3533 is a bright, high-redshift quasar at z=5.9012 [arXiv:1703.02539, arXiv:1806.05691] that is completely devoid of broad emission lines [arXiv:1703.02539]. It hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1806.05691] and exhibits CO(6-5) molecular gas emission [arXiv:1002.1561], but lacks detectable 350-micron dust continuum emission [arXiv:1002.1561].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J1335+3533 as a bright weak-emission-line quasar with a very small proximity zone, precise CO host redshift, and no BAL features.

quotes.quote: The proximity zones of two objects, i.e. CFHQS J2229+1457 and SDSS J1335+3533 , are R_ p < 1 pMpc and R_ p, corr 1 pMpc.

SDSS J1335+3533 This quasar, that has been discovered in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) by Fan2004 , has a precise redshift measurement of z=5.9012 from CO (6-5) emission from the host galaxy Wang2010 , and is fairly bright with a magnitude of M_ 1450 =-26.67 Banados2016 . The measured proximity zone size for this object is R_p=0.78 0.15 pMpc and the luminosity corrected size of the proximity zone is R_ p, corr =0.89 0.17 pMpc. The redshift uncertainty of v=100 km/s results in an uncertainty of the proximity zone measurement of R_p 0.15 pMpc. These measurements of the proximity zone are again independent on continuum modeling uncertainties.

The whole spectrum of the quasar shown in the middle panel of Fig. fig:spectra_small is completely devoid of broad emission lines, as previously noticed from its discovery spectrum by Fan2004 . The spectrum does not show any BAL features.

sources.summary: The paper presents the first detailed spectroscopy of SDSS J1335+3533, finding a very short quasar lifetime, massive black hole, moderate Eddington ratio, and unusually weak emission lines.

quotes.quote: We conduct the first comprehensive spectroscopic study of the youngest quasar known, SDSS J1335+3533 at z=5.9012 , whose lifetime is t_ Q <10^4 yr ( 95 confidence). A careful search of our deep optical and near-infrared spectra for H 1 and metal absorption lines allows us to convincingly exclude the possibility that its small proximity zone results from an associated absorption system rather than a short lifetime. We use the emission line to measure the mass of its black hole to be M_ BH =(4.09 0.58) 10^9 M_ , implying an Eddington ratio of 0.30 0.04 - comparable to other co-eval quasars of similar luminosity. We similarly find that the relationship between its black hole mass and dynamical mass are consistent with the scaling relations measured from other z 6 quasars. The only possible anomaly associated with SDSS J1335+3533 's youth are its weak emission lines, but larger samples are needed to shed light on a potential causality.

One of those young quasars SDSS J1335+3533 (hereafter J1335+3533 ) at z = 5.9012 , exhibits a particularly small proximity zone, i.e. R_p = 0.78 0.15 pMpc, shown as the red dashed line in Fig. fig:life , implying a quasar lifetime of only t_ Q 10^4 yr. In this paper we conduct the first detailed spectroscopic study of the young quasar J1335+3533 .

sources.summary: The paper detects CO(6-5) in J1335+3533, measuring a 310 km/s line width, integrated line intensity, and non-detections in millimeter and 350 micron continuum.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: peaking at the position of 13^ h 35^ m 50.75^ s , 35 33'15.8''

quotes.quote: In this work, we present new observations of CO (6-5) and/or (5-4) line emission in another six z 6 quasars (J0840+5624, J1044 0125, J1048+4637, J1335+3533, J1425+3254, J2054 0005), using the Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI). They all have 250 GHz flux density of S_ 250GHz 1.8 mJy and corresponding FIR luminosities of L_ FIR >5 10^ 12 L_ (using the estimation given in Wang et al. 2008a and 2008b).

The CO (6-5) line was detected in this source, peaking at the position of 13^ h 35^ m 50.75^ s , 35 33'15.8'' , 0.7'' away from the optical quasar position. The CO FWHM fitted with a single Gaussian profile is 310 50 km s ^ 1 . The integrated line intensity is 0.53 0.07 Jy km s ^ 1 , and the 3 upper limit on the continuum emission is 0.15 mJy at 100 GHz. No 350 m dust continuum is detected in our SHARC-II observations of this object.

gmw_00000598.name: J1425+3254
gmw_00000598.overview: J1425+3254 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1002.1561]. Observations of the source have detected CO(6-5) line emission with a broad line width of 690 km/s, though its 350-micron dust continuum remains undetected [arXiv:1002.1561].

sources.summary: The paper detects CO(6-5) in J1425+3254, reporting a broad 690 km/s line, measured line flux, and upper limits on millimeter and 350 micron continuum.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: peaking at the position of 14^ h 25^ m 16.26^ s , 32 54'09.3''

quotes.quote: In this work, we present new observations of CO (6-5) and/or (5-4) line emission in another six z 6 quasars (J0840+5624, J1044 0125, J1048+4637, J1335+3533, J1425+3254, J2054 0005), using the Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI). They all have 250 GHz flux density of S_ 250GHz 1.8 mJy and corresponding FIR luminosities of L_ FIR >5 10^ 12 L_ (using the estimation given in Wang et al. 2008a and 2008b).

The CO (6-5) line was detected, peaking at the position of 14^ h 25^ m 16.26^ s , 32 54'09.3'' , 0.6'' away from the optical quasar. The CO FWHM fitted with a single Gaussian profile is 690 180 km s ^ 1 , and the line flux of 0.59 0.11 Jy km s ^ 1 . The 3 upper limit for the continuum at 101 GHz is 0.12 mJy. This source was not detected in our SHARC-II observations of the 350 m dust continuum with a 1 rms of 2.6 mJy beam ^ 1 .

gmw_00000599.name: J2054 0005
gmw_00000599.overview: J2054 0005 is a high-redshift quasar host galaxy [arXiv:1002.1561]. Observations of the system have detected CO(6-5) line emission with a line width of 360 km/s, while placing an upper limit on the 98 GHz continuum emission [arXiv:1002.1561].

sources.summary: The paper detects CO(6-5) in J2054 0005, measuring line width and intensity while placing a 98 GHz continuum upper limit.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: peaking at the position of 20^ h 54^ m 06.45^ s , 00 05'13.1''

quotes.quote: In this work, we present new observations of CO (6-5) and/or (5-4) line emission in another six z 6 quasars (J0840+5624, J1044 0125, J1048+4637, J1335+3533, J1425+3254, J2054 0005), using the Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI). They all have 250 GHz flux density of S_ 250GHz 1.8 mJy and corresponding FIR luminosities of L_ FIR >5 10^ 12 L_ (using the estimation given in Wang et al. 2008a and 2008b).

The CO (6-5) line was detected in this source, peaking at the position of 20^ h 54^ m 06.45^ s , 00 05'13.1'' , 1.8'' away from the optical quasar. The CO line FWHM obtained from a fit to a single-Gaussian line profile is 360 110 km s ^ 1 . The derived integrated intensity is 0.34 0.07 Jy km s ^ 1 , and the 3 upper limit on the continuum emission at 98 GHz is 0.15 mJy.

gmw_00000600.name: host galaxy of GRB 130528A
gmw_00000600.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 130528A is a luminous galaxy located at a redshift of 1.250 [arXiv:1404.0939]. It features an aged stellar population of approximately 2.6 Gyr with low dust extinction and an estimated star formation rate of 6.18 solar masses per year [arXiv:1404.0939].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 130528A host at redshift 1.250, measuring optical/nIR photometry, [OII]-based star formation, and an old luminous stellar population with low global extinction.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy photometric observations in optical to near-infrared (nIR) wavelengths were achieved through large ground-based aperture telescopes, such as 10.4m Gran Telescopio Canarias (GTC), 4.2m William Herschel Telescope (WHT), 6m Bolshoi Teleskop Alt-azimutalnyi (BTA) telescope, and 2m Liverpool Telescope (LT). Based on these observations, spectral energy distributions (SED) for the host galaxy and afterglow were constructed. Thanks to millimetre (mm) observations at PdBI, we confirm the presence of a mm source within the XRT error circle that faded over the course of our observations and identify the host galaxy.

Spectroscopic observation of this galaxy by GTC showed a single faint emission line that likely corresponds to [ OII ] 3727 at a redshift of 1.250 0.001, implying a star formation rate (SFR)( M_ /yr) 6.18 M_ /yr without correcting for dust extinction.
The SED of the host galaxy is explained well ( ^ 2 / d.o.f. =0.564) by a luminous (M _ B =-21.16), low-extinction ( A_ V =0, rest frame), and aged (2.6 Gyr) stellar population.

gmw_00000601.name: brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in RBS 797
aliases_external: BCG of RBS 797 / RBS 797 central galaxy
gmw_00000601.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy in RBS 797 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2310.07773] that hosts two compact radio components at its center [arXiv:1308.5851, arXiv:1504.02681]. It features two orthogonal pairs of radio jets [arXiv:1308.5851, arXiv:2310.07773] with lobes that are co-spatial with matching X-ray cavities [arXiv:2310.07773].

sources.summary: The paper reports EVN and VLA observations of the BCG in RBS 797, detecting compact radio components and misaligned jets interpreted as possible evidence for a supermassive binary black hole system.

quotes.quote: We aim to reveal the nuclear radio properties of the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in RBS 797 and to investigate the presence of a SMBBH system in its center. We performed new high-resolution observations at 5 GHz with the European VLBI Network (EVN) on May 3, 2013, reaching an angular resolution of 9 5 mas ^2 and a sensitivity of 36 Jy/beam. We also re-analyzed VLA archival data at 4.8 GHz in A- and B- configurations. We report the EVN detection of two compact components in the BCG of RBS 797, with a projected separation of 77 pc.

Our re-analysis of VLA archival data seems to favor the first scenario, as we detect two pairs of radio jets misaligned by 90 degrees on the same kpc scale emanating from the central radio core. If the two outbursts are almost contemporaneous, this is clear evidence of the presence of two active supermassive black holes whose radio nuclei are unresolved at VLA resolution. The nature of the double source detected by our EVN observations in the BCG of RBS 797 can be established only by future sensitive, multi-frequency VLBI observations.

sources.summary: The paper reports orthogonal kpc-scale radio jets and two compact VLBI components in the RBS 797 BCG, interpreting them as evidence for an SMBH binary scenario.

quotes.quote: The radio emission from the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in RBS 797 (z=0.35) observed at VLA resolutions shows different orientations of the radio jets and lobes with scale Gitti_2006 . In particular, the kpc-scale radio jets oriented to the north-south (N-S) direction are almost orthogonal to the axis of the diffuse radio emission filling the X-ray cavities on ten-kpc scale (Figure rbs797.fig , left panel). On the other hand, from a recent re-analysis of archival VLA data at 4.8 GHz, we find strong evidence of the presence of kpc-scale jets emanating also to the east-west (E-W) direction (Figure rbs797.fig , middle panel), indicating that the radio-emitting plasma on the E-W direction is still being freshly injected.

Since no information on the pc-scale radio properties are available in literature, we performed an explorative program to assess the detectability of the BCG in RBS 797 at VLBI resolution and to study its nuclear region. In particular, on 3 May 2013 we conducted a test observation (PI: M. Gitti) in the e-VLBI run with a subset of the European VLBI Network (EVN). The total time spent on the target was about 1 hour. As it appears evident from the 5 GHz EVN map (Figure rbs797.fig , right panel), we clearly detected two compact components.

sources.summary: The paper describes RBS 797's central BCG as a powerful AGN with orthogonal jet pairs, radio lobes, and matching X-ray cavity systems.

quotes.quote: Chandra observations revealed the presence of two X-ray cavities Schindler2001 located at the north-east and south-west (east-west cavities) with respect to the central brightest cluster galaxy (BCG). The central BCG is a powerful AGN, which has been studied in the radio band by Gitti2006,2011cavagnolo,Doria2012,Gitti13 . These studies have detected two jets from the central AGN, oriented in the east-west direction.

The lobes associated with these jets are co-spatial with the cavities detected in the X-rays. A second pair of jets has been detected, departing from the AGN and oriented north-south, that is, orthogonal with respect to the east-west lobes. Recently, deep Chandra observations have revealed a second pair of cavities in the north-south direction, co-spatial with the north-south lobes of the AGN.

gmw_00000602.name: AO 0235+164
gmw_00000602.overview: AO 0235+164 is a highly variable blazar at a redshift of z = 0.94 [arXiv:1405.7694, arXiv:0801.1236] that is historically classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2101.02497], though it exhibits some flat-spectrum radio quasar characteristics [arXiv:2403.13713]. The source features a compact jet geometry [arXiv:1805.04299] and is situated in a complex environment with interacting foreground galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0608627].

sources.summary: The paper examines the 1993 radio flare of 0235+164, constructing simultaneous spectra and finding its flux evolution follows the shock model without a clear plateau.

quotes.quote: We also studied one individual source, 0235+164 , and its flare in 1993 in more detail. Figure 0235 a, shows the flux density curve of the source at 37 GHz, with every flare analysed for this source marked using arrows. Fig. 0235 b shows the 1993 flare in more detail at 37 GHz. We have marked seven epochs of the flare and constructed simultaneous spectra at every epoch at all the frequencies used in the analysis (Fig. 0235 ).

The spectra are simultaneous within two weeks but the 230 GHz points are in general more uncertain because of the more sparse sampling. The flare follows the shock model quite well with the flux density first rising at all frequencies. When the flux density starts to decay the peak of the spectrum moves to lower frequencies. No clear plateau is seen in this flare which agrees with the general results for the whole sample.

sources.summary: The paper treats AO 0235+164 as an active LBL-type blazar and constrains its gamma-ray emitting region to lie outside the BLR near dusty-torus radiation.

quotes.quote: ht AO 0235+164 at MJD 54760 AO 0235+164 ( z = 0.94 , d_ L 6.14 Gpc ) is an LBL-type blazar, which was active in 2008--2009. The highest gamma-ray state, achieved between MJD 54700 and MJD 54780, was analyzed in detail by . They estimated the observed gamma-ray luminosity as L_ 6.7 10^ 47 erg s^ 1 ; the observed variability time scale t_ var,obs 3 d = 2.6 10^5 s ; the Compton dominance q = L_ /L_ syn 4 ; the synchrotron to X-ray luminosity ratio L_ syn /L_ X 6 ; the accretion disk luminosity L_ d = 4 10^ 45 erg s^ 1 ; and the characteristic radii of external radiation components r_ BLR 0.06 pc and r_ IR 1.6 pc . For the black hole mass, they adopted M_ BH 4 10^8 M_ .

In Figure fig_ao0235 , we plot the constraints on the location of the gamma-ray flare, adopting _ BLR = _ IR = 0.1 . The marginal solution is located at r_ min 0.65 pc and _ min 22 . The predictions for this solution are _ SSA,obs 920 m , u_ '/u_ B ' 0.7 , and L_ j,min 8.5 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 0.2 L_ d . The gamma-ray emitting region is certainly located outside the BLR, in the region where external radiation is dominated by the dusty torus emission.

sources.summary: The paper finds this blazar has the sample's strongest emission lines, confirms z=0.942, detects Mg II, oxygen, and H, and argues its strong infrared H warrants FSRQ reclassification.

quotes.quote: BZBJ0238+1636 (AO 0235+164) This is the blazar featuring the most prominent emission lines in our sample. We confirm the redshift of this source to be 0.942 0.001 Healey+08 . Five lines have been detected, namely, the Mg II doublet, [ O II ] , the [ O III ] doublet and H . The H line falls in a telluric region, so only a shallow upper limit could be set. The H line rest-frame EW is higher than 5 .

All the other features detected in the optical band have instead values <5 . BZBJ0238+1636, previously classified as a BL Lac due to the EW of its optical lines, should be re-classified as an FSRQ according to its strong H feature in the IR band. The detected emission lines are displayed in Fig. fig2 .

sources.summary: The paper describes AO 0235+16 as a z=0.94 blazar with extreme emission variations and more than 5 mag optical brightness changes, finding optical/radio variability scales near 11.2 and 5.7 years.

quotes.quote: The source AO 0235+16 is a well known blazar at z=0.94 that exhibits spectacular emission variations on both short ( day) and long (months, years) time scales. It was classified as a BL Lac object by Spinrad Smith ( ), who observed a 2 mag variation. Noticeable optical outbursts were described by Rieke et al. \ ( ), Pica et al. \ ( ), Webb et al. \ ( ), Webb Smith ( ), Webb et al. \ ( ). From the data reported in the literature, an overall brightness variation of more than 5 mag can be seen, which makes AO 0235+16 one of the most interesting sources for variability studies.

Discrete autocorrelation function analysis of the optical and radio light curves points out a characteristic time scale for the flux variability of AO 0235+16 of about 11.2 years. However, a deeper insight into the optical and radio light curves aided by DFT analysis and folded light curves reveals that major outbursts occur at roughly half the above time scale, even if one of the events (the 1982 one) appears noticeably delayed. Indeed, in the best sampled 8.0 GHz light curve, there are five large-amplitude outbursts whose peaks are separated by 2308 , 1870 , 2007 , and 2091 days, giving an average ``period" of 2069 184 days, i.e. \ 5.7 0.5 years.
